Join our core backend team where your pipelines and data solutions will enable Finance teams to operate with speed, accuracy, and insight—ultimately supporting our mission to change lives for millions of patients.

Your new role

As a Data Engineer in our core backend team, you'll build and operate the scalable data infrastructure that underpins our Finance Data Centre. Working primarily with Databricks, you'll build robust ETL/ELT solutions that transform raw data into reliable, timely insights for our global Finance organization. This is a hands-on technical role where your work directly enables finance teams to make data-driven decisions that matter.

Your Day-to-day Responsibilities Will Include

  • Building, implementing, and operating scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT solutions in Databricks and Azure Data Factory
  • Working with raw data, source systems, data modelling, transformation logic, and orchestration to ensure timely and accurate finance data delivery
  • Implementing best practices for data quality, observability, performance, and cost optimization in a cloud environment
  • Supporting timely deployment of new features, solutions, changes,
  • Operational support, production incident resolution to maintain high system reliability
  • Collaborating with product owners, analysts, and architects to translate business requirements into technical solutions that deliver measurable business value
  • Ensuring data governance, security, and compliance across all data engineering solutions
  • Working in agile, cross-functional teams with a focus on delivering production-ready, high-quality solutions

Your new department

You'll join a department, where we deliver a robust and scalable data foundation that enables the global Finance organization to operate efficiently and make insight-driven decisions. Our Finance Data Centre is strengthening its capabilities across our core technology stack—SAP BW/4HANA, Databricks, Azure and Microsoft Fabric. You'll work alongside solution architects, developers, analysts, and data engineers from diverse technical and multinational backgrounds. We design, build, and maintain data solutions that support core finance processes, with collaboration, business focus, and technical excellence at the heart of how we work. Our goal is to use data to gain new understandings, drive optimizations, and create true business value for Finance, Novo Nordisk, and the patients we serve.

In Finance, Legal & Global Solutions, we enable Novo Nordisk to fulfil our purpose by safeguarding the business, providing corporate infrastructure, and driving value creation. We are a team of 2,300 colleagues primarily based at our headquarters in Denmark and our Global Business Services centres in India and Mexico.

Your Skills & Qualifications

We're looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong hands-on expertise in building production-grade data pipelines. You will work in cross-functional and multidisciplinary teams to deliver high-quality, production-ready solutions. You will furthermore collaborate with product owners, analysts and architects to translate business requirements into technical solutions that deliver measurable business value. Hence, bringing a collaborative mindset, understanding the business we support and engaging with stakeholders is a prerequisite.

You'll Thrive In This Role If You Bring

  • Strong hands-on experience with Databricks and Unity Catalog (Spark, Delta Lake, notebooks, jobs)
  • Proficiency in Python, SQL, and familiarity with Data Engineering frameworks
  • Experience with data modelling, ingestion patterns, and orchestration tools
  • Experience with dimensional modelling
  • Knowledge of cloud platform services (Azure/AWS/GCP) relevant to Databricks
  • Experience with data observability tools, infrastructure as code, and DevOps practices
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate finance needs into technical solutions
  • A collaborative mindset and willingness to work in agile, cross-functional teams
  • Strong quality and delivery focus, with the ability to produce robust, production-ready solutions
  • Commitment to data governance, security, and compliance
  • Fluent spoken and written English

Nice To Have

  • Experiences with Genie code or other relevant experiences for implementing skills
  • Interest in finance data and core finance processes — you'll work closely with Finance stakeholders, so business curiosity is valuable
  • Experience working in a regulated industry or financial organization

Working at Novo Nordisk

We are a proud life-science company, and life is our reason to exist. We’re inspired by life in all its forms and shapes, ups and downs, opportunities and challenges. For employees at Novo Nordisk, life means many things – from the building blocks of life that form the basis of ground-breaking scientific research, to our rich personal lives that motivate and energise us to perform our best at work. Ultimately, life is why we’re all here - to ensure that people can lead a life independent of chronic disease.

Deadline

24 May 2026

Please note that we will be assessing applications as we go, so we recommend applying as soon as possible.

Please apply in English and refrain from adding a photo to your CV to ensure fair and efficient processing. We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all applicants.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: Data Engineer - Team Data Foundation
Description:
Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

What's in it for you?

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.

International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

Who are you ideally?


You think of data not just as pipelines, but as a product. You care about who consumes the data - whether it's a customer running queries in their workspace or an internal team building on top of your platform - and you design with their experience in mind.
You bring a strong background in data engineering and are proficient in Python, SQL, and Spark. From streaming ingestion to historical backfills, from performance tuning to choosing the right partitioning strategy - these are the kinds of challenges that motivate you.
You have hands-on experience with Databricks and Delta Lake, or similar lakehouse technologies.
You understand the medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) and know how to model data for different consumption patterns.
You're curious about how AI and large language models can be enabled on top of large-scale data - and you're excited to help build the foundation that makes that possible.
Experience with Kafka, multi-tenant data architectures, or IoT/telematics data is a strong plus.


Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Primary Responsibilities

Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.

Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.

Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.

Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.

Our hiring process

Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.

Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team.

Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.

If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.

Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

Coming Together To Connect Construction

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.

The question is: Are you in?

Title: Data Scientist (Python & SQL) - Freelance AI Trainer
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

  • Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
  • Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
  • Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
  • Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
  • Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
  • Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
  • Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • 5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
  • Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
  • Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
  • Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
  • Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
  • Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
  • Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
  • Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
  • Strong written English (C1+)

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Junior BI Analyst
Description:
Are you our new Junior BI Analyst with a passion for data, business support, and developing Insight?

At DESMI, we are strengthening our data and analytics capabilities to support better decision-making across the organization. We are looking for a curious and analytical Junior BI Analyst who thrives on transforming data into meaningful insights and supporting the business with high-quality analysis. In this role, you will play a key part in running our BI support function, ensuring that stakeholders across DESMI have access to reliable data, insightful reporting, and clear answers to their business questions.

DESMI is a dynamic and innovative company where collaboration, continuous learning, and professional development are at the core of our culture. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with modern BI tools, work closely with stakeholders across functions, and contribute to strengthening our data-driven culture.

A glimpse into the role

As our Junior BI Analyst, you will be the responsible for delivering business intelligence support and analysis. You will work closely with colleagues across departments, helping them understand data, answer key business questions, and translate requirements into impactful Power BI solutions.

You will be part of a dedicated Finance & BI environment and collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders, contributing to the continued development of our DESMI Insights platform.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include


Running the BI support function: Act as the first point of contact for BI-related questions, supporting users and ensuring they get value from our data and reports
Developing Power BI reports: Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide clear and actionable insights
Delivering business analysis: Conduct thorough and structured analyses to support decision-making across the organization
Translating business needs into data solutions: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their requirements and turn them into scalable BI solutions
Ensuring data quality: Validate and reconcile data to ensure consistency, reliability, and trust in reporting
Supporting continuous improvement: Contribute to developing and optimizing our BI platform, data models, and reporting standards Your next opportunity starts here


We are looking for a structured and curious analyst who enjoys diving into data and turning complexity into clarity. You are motivated by understanding the business behind the numbers and delivering insights that make a real impact.

We also imagine that you:


Hold a master’s degree in economics, business administration (e.g. Cand.merc), with a strong understanding of business strategy or performance management.
Have a solid understanding of how data supports business decision-making, including financial and operational metrics
Have experience with or a strong interest in Power BI both analysis and report development
Are analytical and detail-oriented, with the ability to perform thorough and well-documented analyses
Are proactive and service-minded, enjoying close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization
Communicate complex data insights in a clear and understandable way
Have a structured approach to problem-solving and a strong sense of ownership


Whether you are at the beginning of your career or have gained some initial experience, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop your skills in a global organization and become a key player in driving data-driven decision-making at DESMI.

The perks of being at DESMI


Flexible working hours – including possibilities for remote work, but on-site attendance is encouraged
Global career prospects – work with colleagues across Europe, Asia, and the U.S.
Skills development and career growth – Training, job rotation, and internal mobility
Pension – attractive employer contributions
Private healthcare and life insurance
On-site canteen – light breakfast, lunch buffet, and snacks
Company sports & social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties


What We’re About

But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.

When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect


A collaborative and supportive team environment.
Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
A company that values sustainability and innovation.


We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.

Ready to apply?

If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

We are looking for a dedicated Master Data Analyst to join our dynamic team!

In this role, you will play a critical part in managing and securing data accuracy across our global production facilities, ensuring we have the right information at the right time to support planning and production.


Core Responsibilities

Being a part of the team, you will have a deep understanding of the product lifecycle process from development to production and phase out. More specifically you will





Complete core operational Master Data tasks in ERP System such as Bill of Material creation and maintenance, Work Center, Routing and Production versions of core line components and products
Handle data of material movements between production sites to support planning requests and load levelling together with master data updates for the best optimized planning
Complete reports for data-validation to enable data governance and high quality
Collaborate with partners handling associated and supporting tasks to master data processes, especially Planning- and Production sites and Novelty Development
Participate as a collaborator in tasks of new initiatives and developments of products that has an impact towards the existing master data structure
Help optimize the way we work to enable execution of tasks with high quality and speed including building upon the development of Master Data functionalities and needs for the future




Play your part in our team succeeding

You will join the Supply Chain Foundation Master Data team, consisting of 10 dedicated employees located in Billund. Data quality is our top priority as Master Data is critical to the full Supply Chain and Planning Operations. With global standards we focus on process alignment related to Master Data; all in all to keep up a high data quality as a prerequisite for stable planning processes.

We are the interface between marketing novelty projects and planning/manufacturing with a central role in the organization to provide the foundation for Supply Chain Planning. You will also play a role to secure knowledge on master data processes and systems are applied in new initiatives – balancing the customers needs with our obligation to safeguard data structure and simplicity.

Do you have what it takes?





Your educational background could be Production Engineer or Master in Supply Chain Management. However, the educational background is secondary to the right person and experience
Experience with Microsoft Office programs especially Excel is a requirement
Experience with SAP as ERP system is a strong value for the job
Experience within master data, supply chain, quality, production planning etc. and knowhow from a production company is an advantage
Proficiency in English both written and spoken
Thrives with a combination of new tasks and daily operational tasks characterized by routines
Very detail oriented, structured and have a systematic and analytical way of working
Curious to new perspectives and brave to challenge known processes
The ability to see the end2end perspective and you continuously focus on growing your functional expertise




Please note that relocation support is not offered for this role.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Student Worker - Content and Editorial
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Do you want to join a Content and Editorial team that's striving to provide inspirational experiences for kids across our owned platforms (apps, web, magazine)?

We are looking for someone who’s had exposure to digital content, Magazine content and copywriting with a proactive mindset that has attention to detail and a creative and collaborative attitude.


Core Responsibilities





Content management including but not limited to, content planning, content publishing and driving quality assurance of live content.
Keeping a close eye on the community in the LEGO Play App and reporting on content performance and trends.
Proofing of our LEGO Magazine and handling of UGC submissions.
Creating adhoc content or copy where needed.
Bringing enthusiasm and ideas to creative brainstorms.
Staying up to date with landscape trends and insights from our competitors to inspire the team with new ideas for content, games and events – especially for the 8-12 year old audience.




Play you part in our team suceeding

The Content & Editorial team within Digital Consumer Engagement is responsible for strategizing and producing content for our owned channels that are built in-house. These platforms allow The LEGO Group to offer inspirational, personal and social experiences to kids and adults across all touch points. This includes the LEGO® Play App and magazine, LEGO.com/kids, LEGO.com as well as several new innovative platforms currently under construction. Our team tries to bridge the gap between our software engineering and UX design teams creating these platforms on the one side and the product teams creating marketing campaigns for our franchises on the other. Together we create campaign and engagement content for our Digital Ecosystem that ties together all digital LEGO experiences in meaningful ways, creating deeper consumer engagement and improved consumer life-time value.

Do you have what it takes?





Student at Bachelor or Master level.
Degrees in the fields of Digital Design, Communication, Marketing will be beneficial.
Has an interest in LEGO, digital platforms, the latest digital trends and content management.
Wants to work in a fast-paced agile environment.
Has attention to detail and a systematic approach to challenges.
Eager to learn and be part of a global team.
An ambitious self-starter who will take initiative.
Has experience in communication or digital design (this is a plus, though not mandatory).
English at fluent level is required as this is the standard office language. Other languages are a positive plus.




Nice to have





Thrives and contributes in a team setting.
Previous experience working with content management systems (CMS) or on social media, gaming or other online platforms – both professionally or in their spare time.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Entry Associate
Place: Søborg, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
TFS HealthScience is a leading global mid-size Contract Research Organization (CRO) that partners with biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies throughout their entire clinical development journey. Our expertise includes full-service capabilities, resourcing, and Functional Service (FSP) solutions.

As part of our SRS/FSP team, you will be dedicated to one sponsor, a global pharmaceutical or biotechnology company that is an industry leader within its therapeutic area.

Key Responsibilities


Data entry into eCRF (electronic Case Report Form).
Query resolution support.
Document management support.
Monitoring visit preparation.
Audit readiness support.


Qualifications


EDC / eCRF system experience (Medidata Rave, Viedoc, Oracle InForm).
GCP awareness — full certification preferred.
Attention to detail and query resolution experience.
Clinical trial or healthcare data background preferred.
Local language mandatory.
Comfortable working fractional (0.25 FTE) across multiple sites.


What We Offer

We provide opportunities for personal and professional growth in a rewarding environment. You will join a team that values collaboration, quality, and making a difference in the lives of patients.

Our core values of Trust, Quality, Passion, Flexibility, and Sustainability guide our decisions and shape our culture. By aligning on these values, we foster collaboration, innovation, and a shared commitment to excellence. Together, we make a difference.

A Bit More About Us

Our journey began over 30 years ago in Sweden, in the city of Lund. As a full-service global CRO, we build solution-driven teams working toward a healthier future. Bringing together over 600 professionals, TFS delivers tailored clinical research services in more than 50 countries, offering flexible clinical development and strategic resourcing solutions across key therapeutic areas including Dermatology, Neuroscience, Oncology, and Ophthalmology.

Title: Finance Student Job position

Position

Do you wish to join the House of Dreams?

In this House, you will continue to perpetuate the dream of our creator: that of a more beautiful and happier world.

You will also consolidate our vision: to become the most desirable House in the world of beauty, an absolute reference of French luxury, a symbol of joy and hope.

Excel for ultimate luxury

Cultivate Triumphant inclusivity

Commit and act to re-enchant the world.

Design your dreams with Dior.

Your Impact

Within Finance team, the Finance Student Assistant is responsible for operational processes.

This role is crucial for learn the operational processes around financial planning, analysis and controlling.

The holder of this position will work closely with various functional partners, including the Nordic Controlling Manager.

Position


Performing weekly sales reconciliation between retailers’ cashier and Dior cashier on DTC business.
Controlling and approving expenses related to Marketing.
Follow up of Actuals vs Forecasted expenses related to Marketing.
Supervising Cycle Counts in DTC doors.
Creating new analyzing tools (mainly on excel) to improve efficiency.

The responsibilities outlined in this job description are subject to change based on the company's needs and ongoing projects

Title: Butiksassistent - 20 timer - Odense
Description:
Introduktion

Vil du være med til at give vores kunder en indkøbsoplevelse udover det sædvanlige? Så er du måske vores nye butiksassistent!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold, og vi har et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, når det kommer til dagligvareindkøb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen – med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig. Derfor investerer vi i dig som medarbejder. Og derfor er der ingen grænser for, hvor langt du kan nå.

Som butiksassistent i Lidl får du ansvarsfulde opgaver og mulighed for at gøre en positiv forskel for vores kunder. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, ligesom du får adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt. Og det gælder for alle i Lidl – uanset om du arbejder fuldtid eller deltid.

Om jobbet

Det allervigtigste i dit job bliver at sørge for, at vores kunder får en indkøbsoplevelse ud over det sædvanlige. Du kan både tale med Hr. Jensen på hans daglige besøg i butikken, og give den travle småbørnsfamilie et smil med på vejen. Dine opgaver bliver blandt andet at:


Give vores kunder fremragende service under hele deres indkøbsoplevelse
Betjene kunderne ved kassen
Sørge for, at butikken altid er flot og skinnende ren
Fylde varer op på hylderne


Om dig

Når du starter i Lidl, får du et grundigt oplæringsforløb, og vi slipper dig ikke løs i butikken, før du har styr på vores processer. Derfor er det ikke afgørende for os, at du har erfaring fra butik. Til gengæld forventer vi, at du:


Er smilende og glad for kundekontakt
Tager initiativ og er hjælpsom
Holder af faste rammer og har fokus på detaljen
Trives i en travl hverdag
Er mødestabil og pligtopfyldende


Vi tilbyder

Som butiksassistent i Lidl får du:


Verdensklasse sammenhold
En konkurrencedygtig løn på 148,03 kr. i timen som faglært og 138,67 kr. i timen som ufaglært
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst fra HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Garanteret udvikling og uddannelse
Gode karrieremuligheder, hvis du har ambitioner om mere
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.


Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Sådan søger du stillingen:


Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV


Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidlvest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0535, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.

Vi ser frem til at modtage dit motiverede CV!

Title: Assistant Manager - Onboarding & Support
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Er du vores nye Assistant Manager i onboarding & support?

Vil du være med til at drive en virksomhed i vækst og tage ansvar for centrale økonomiske og operationelle processer for vores kunder? Har du erfaring med økonomistyring og lyst til at udvikle dig i den rolle?

Så er du måske vores nye Assistant Manager hos PENAW!

Vi er en rådgivnings- og outsourcing-virksomhed med speciale i procesoptimering, digitalisering, rapportering og strategisk sparring. Som Assistant Manager vil du få en central rolle i at understøtte vores kunder og interne teams med indsigt, struktur og fremdrift.




Dine primære ansvarsområder:

Onboarding af nye kunder 
Opsætning af systemer
Afstemninger
Daglig kundekontakt & opfølgning 
Ad hoc-regnskabsopgaver 
Andre administrative support opgaver




Du bliver en del af vores supportafdeling der ligger i Odense. Teamet består på nuværende tidspunkt af 6 personer der varetager både eksterne- og interne support opgaver, samt løn og onboarding af nye kunder. Vi tilbyder stor fleksibilitet, korte beslutningsveje og mulighed for at præge både din rolle og virksomhedens udvikling.




Vi forestiller os, at du:

Har en relevant videregående uddannelse inden for økonomi, finans eller business
Erfaring med økonomistyring og løn, er ikke et krav. 
Er struktureret, analytisk og god til at skabe overblik
Trives med ansvar og har stærke samarbejdsevner
Har kendskab til ERP-systemer og digitale værktøjer
Er initiativrig og har lyst til at bidrage til virksomhedens vækst




Praktisk information:

Stillingen er fuldtid med base på vores kontor i Odense
Mulighed for hjemmearbejde
Tiltrædelse: Hurtigst muligt




Ansøgning:

Send din ansøgning til info@penaw.dk og skriv "Assistant Manager" i emnelinjen. Vi afholder samtaler løbende og ansætter, når den rette kandidat er fundet – så send gerne din ansøgning allerede i dag.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte vores Support Manager Camilla Bottelet på tlf. +45 28 18 80 59 eller mail cb@penaw.dk.




Om PENAW

Vi hjælper SMV’er med at få dyb indsigt i deres forretning og understøtter strategiprocesser og forretningsplaner. Vi fungerer som drifts- og implementeringspartner og sikrer høj kvalitet i alle faser – altid i tæt samarbejde med ledelsen og nøglepersoner.

Vores team består af erfarne profiler med baggrund som CEO, CFO og specialister inden for optimering og projektledelse. Vi arbejder på tværs af brancher og skaber målbare resultater for vores kunder.

Title: Gym Staff - Kennedys Plads
Description:
Jobtitel

Gym Staff - Kennedys Plads

Bliv en del af holdet bag Danmarks største Fitnessfællesskab

Er du er på udkig efter et deltidsjob eller studiejob med mening, eller drømmer du om at starte din karriere indenfor fitness og sundhed, så glæder vi os til at få dig med på teamet hos PureGym Kennedys Plads i Aalborg

Hvorfor blive en del af PureGym?

Hos PureGym ved vi, at vores medarbejdere er vores største styrke. Vi værdsætter din unikke personlighed, dine ideer og din vilje til at skabe og være en del af noget større. Vi arbejder for at skabe en arbejdsplads, hvor du føler dig værdsat, udfordret og inspireret til at vokse – både personligt og professionelt.

Hvis dine fremtidige karriereønsker er indenfor ledelse og fitness har vi karriereveje med interne uddannelser – både via digital læringsplatform og i kurser som holdinstruktør.

Om rollen

Som Gym Staff vil du være PureGyms ansigt udadtil, og være den, der sikrer den gode medlemsoplevelse og det motiverende træningsmiljø. Du vil arbejde både selvstændigt, og tæt sammen med dine kolleger, og din Gym Manager. Dine opgaver vil spænde bredt – fra daglig rengøring af centeret til vedligeholdelse af maskiner. Derudover vil du og dit team være centrets repræsentanter på vores lokale SoMe-kanal og viser stolt PureGyms teal-farve frem i lokalområdet.

Til denne Gym Staff rolle søger vi én til vores team i Kennedys Plads, til 10 timer om ugen. De vagter, som kan blive dine, vil som udgangspunkt være: en hverdagsaften(tirsdag) + hver anden weekenden

Dine Opgaver


Være serviceminded og skabe den gode stemning sammen med vores medlemmer
Hjælpe medlemmer med at navigere i selvbetjeningsløsninger (appen, hjemmeside)
Oprydning, rengøring og vedligeholdelse af udstyr – så centeret altid står skarpt og indbydende
Give vores medlemmer en god start og guide dem i centret i brugen af vores udstyr
Sørge for, at vores salgsautomater er fyldte og klar til medlemskøb
Hjælpe med lagerstyring og lageroptælling
Deltage i centerets SoMe aktiviteter, hvor du også er med på kameraret.
Stå i spidsen for Functional Express, som er træning af en mindre gruppe medlemmer ude på gulvet. Du er med til at formidle øvelser og skabe god energi.
Oplære dine nye kollegaer og samarbejde med instruktører og trænere
Andre ad hoc-opgaver, der sikrer en optimal drift


Dine kvalifikationer

Hvem er du?


Energisk, serviceminded og nysgerrig på at opsøge relationer
Trives med praktiske opgaver såsom rengøring, organisering og vedligeholdelse
Komfortabel med at guide vores medlemmer på digitale platforme (app og hjemmeside)
Kan bidrage til en positiv, inkluderende kultur og være en rollemodel for vores værdier
Kan skabe et både sjovt og trygt miljø
Har gå på mod, er problemløsende og ivrig efter at lære nyt


Vi Tilbyder


En meningsfuld rolle, hvor du gør en forskel for vores medlemmer og bliver en del til Danmarks største fitnessfællesskab, der sikrer et sundere Danmark
Faste arbejdstider der kan ligge både i hverdag og weekend, samt morgen, eftermiddag og aften
Mulighed for personlig udvikling og læring gennem vores trænings- og udviklingsprogrammer, samt holduddannelser – samt en karrievej mod ledelse
En inkluderende kultur, hvor mangfoldighed og kreativitet værdsættes
Et arbejdsmiljø, hvor forandring og feedback er i centrum
Gratis personalemedlemskab så du frit kan træne og deltage på hold i alle PureGym centre i Danmark.


Bliv en del af noget større

Som medarbejder i PureGym bliver du en del af Danmarks største fitnesskæde med over 130 centre fordelt i hele landet. PureGym DK er en del af en af Europas største fitnesskæder, med hovedkontor i London, UK, med centre i Danmark, England, Schweiz, UAE og New York, US.

Vores mission er at inspirere til et sundere liv med ’Fitness for alle’ som omdrejningspunkt. Vi ser os selv som mere end bare en fitnesskæde. Vi hjælper vores medlemmer med at passe på deres fysiske og mentale sundhed i de bedste rammer med professionel support fra vores passionerede medarbejdere.

Ansøgningsfrist

Er du klar til at blive en del af vores team? Vi holder løbende samtaler, så send gerne din ansøgning allerede i dag eller snarest muligt.

Har du nogle spørgsmål er du velkommen til at komme forbi centeret.

Vi glæder os til at læse din ansøgning!

Er vi endt i spam?

Du vil altid modtage en bekræftelsesmail, når du har sendt din ansøgning. Hvis ikke du modtager denne, så tjek dit spamfilter og sørg for at fjerne os derfra, så fremtidig kommunikation lander i din almindelige indbakke.

Kontrakt

Tidsubegrænset

Stillingens lokation

Område

Nordjylland

Center/Afdeling

Aalborg, Kennedy Plads

Rekrutterende part

Startdato

Hurtigst muligt

  • Deltage i centerets SoMe aktiviteter, hvor du også er med på kameraret.
  • Stå i spidsen for Functional Express, som er træning af en mindre gruppe medlemmer ude på gulvet. Du er med til at formidle øvelser og skabe god energi.
  • Oplære dine nye kollegaer og samarbejde med instruktører og trænere
  • Andre ad hoc-opgaver, der sikrer en optimal drift

Dine kvalifikationer

Hvem er du?

  • Energisk, serviceminded og nysgerrig på at opsøge relationer
  • Trives med praktiske opgaver såsom rengøring, organisering og vedligeholdelse
  • Komfortabel med at guide vores medlemmer på digitale platforme (app og hjemmeside)
  • Kan bidrage til en positiv, inkluderende kultur og være en rollemodel for vores værdier
  • Kan skabe et både sjovt og trygt miljø
  • Har gå på mod, er problemløsende og ivrig efter at lære nyt

Vi Tilbyder

  • En meningsfuld rolle, hvor du gør en forskel for vores medlemmer og bliver en del til Danmarks største fitnessfællesskab, der sikrer et sundere Danmark
  • Faste arbejdstider der kan ligge både i hverdag og weekend, samt morgen, eftermiddag og aften
  • Mulighed for personlig udvikling og læring gennem vores trænings- og udviklingsprogrammer, samt holduddannelser – samt en karrievej mod ledelse
  • En inkluderende kultur, hvor mangfoldighed og kreativitet værdsættes
  • Et arbejdsmiljø, hvor forandring og feedback er i centrum
  • Gratis personalemedlemskab så du frit kan træne og deltage på hold i alle PureGym centre i Danmark.

Bliv en del af noget større

Som medarbejder i PureGym bliver du en del af Danmarks største fitnesskæde med over 130 centre fordelt i hele landet. PureGym DK er en del af en af Europas største fitnesskæder, med hovedkontor i London, UK, med centre i Danmark, England, Schweiz, UAE og New York, US.

Vores mission er at inspirere til et sundere liv med ’Fitness for alle’ som omdrejningspunkt. Vi ser os selv som mere end bare en fitnesskæde. Vi hjælper vores medlemmer med at passe på deres fysiske og mentale sundhed i de bedste rammer med professionel support fra vores passionerede medarbejdere.

Ansøgningsfrist

Er du klar til at blive en del af vores team? Vi holder løbende samtaler, så send gerne din ansøgning allerede i dag eller snarest muligt.

Har du nogle spørgsmål er du velkommen til at komme forbi centeret.

Vi glæder os til at læse din ansøgning!

Er vi endt i spam?

Du vil altid modtage en bekræftelsesmail, når du har sendt din ansøgning. Hvis ikke du modtager denne, så tjek dit spamfilter og sørg for at fjerne os derfra, så fremtidig kommunikation lander i din almindelige indbakke.

Title: Senior Business Analyst – Data, Insights & AI
Description:
Job Description

As a Senior Business Analyst in Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department, you are the key link between the business and our technology. You turn complex business challenges into data- and AI-enabled solutions, drive discovery and value framing, and ensure that what we build delivers measurable impact for Falck’s customers, patients, and operations.

You will work across Business Units and teams, shaping how we use data, analytics, automation, and AI to advance sustainable healthcare and emergency response.

We are looking for a T-shaped profile – someone with deep expertise in business analysis who is also continuously expanding their skills across data, analytics, and AI as technologies and ways of working evolve. You will join a growing community of 60+ data and AI professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw. Your focus is to find the right problems, define the right solutions, and ensure they are adopted and deliver value.

Your Main Responsibilities Will Involve


Identifying and framing high-impact business problems where data, analytics, automation, and AI can create strategic value as well as looking beyond the initial request to understand the underlying business need, challenge assumptions, and reframe problems to maximize value.
Building ROI-based business cases: articulate expected costs, benefits, and risks; quantify value (e.g., revenue uplift, cost savings, efficiency, quality, experience).
Acting as a trusted business partner to senior stakeholders across multiple business units.
Facilitating workshops, interviews, and working sessions to align on problems, priorities, and solution options.
Translating business problems into clear, testable requirements (epics, user stories, acceptance criteria) plus translate business concepts into logical and conceptual data models, defining how entities, relationships, measures, and KPIs should be represented in the data platform.
Collaborating daily with Engineers to validate feasibility and data availability, co-design solution approaches, and refine requirements as learning emerges.
Owning the business side of the solution lifecycle – from discovery to adoption: requirements and design, testing and validation, business readiness and go-live, and post-launch optimization.
Leading or coordinating User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including designing test cases that match real-world scenarios and edge cases.
Mentoring and support more junior team members, especially colleagues working with business analysis and insights, sharing best practices and providing feedback on complex challenges.
Being recognized as a go-to domain expert in your business area and share lessons learned across teams and projects.


About youExperience & track record


Several years of experience as a Business Analyst preferably in data, analytics, digital, or AI-driven environments.
Proven track record of delivering measurable business outcomes through applied analytics, automation, or AI initiatives.
Demonstrated experience in end-to-end delivery – from discovery and business case, through requirements and solution design, to testing, deployment, adoption, and benefit tracking.
Solid experience with stakeholder management at senior levels, including navigating conflicting priorities and driving alignment.
Strong problem-structuring and problem-solving skills.
Ability to break down complex challenges into clear, prioritized increments.
Experience writing high-quality user stories, use cases, and acceptance criteria.
Ability to convert data into insight narratives that drive decisions.
Familiarity with AI and ML concepts
Work with technical teams on data modelling approaches, helping to shape how business concepts are implemented in our data lakehouse architecture and in dimensional models so analytics and insights are trustworthy and easy to consume.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken; ability to make complex and “black box” topics understandable and actionable.
Strong facilitation skills for workshops, interviews, and cross-functional sessions.
Curious and continuously learning – especially about AI, data, and customer behaviour.
Collaborative – you work in cross-functional groups and share knowledge openly.
Structured and able to manage multiple parallel initiatives without losing sight of detail or big picture.


About us

Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department brings together experts in data engineering, analytics, AI, automation, and business partnering to support a global healthcare and emergency services organisation. With 60+ professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw, we combine deep technical expertise with strong business understanding to build data and AI solutions that are reliable, scalable, and actually used.

We work closely across the business and with central technology teams, including a mission-critical Data Platform team that provides shared standards and frameworks to accelerate delivery. We invest in our people, encourage T-shaped development, and foster a culture of care, trust, and collaboration – turning care into action through data and AI.

About Us

At Falck we build on a legacy of more than one hundred years in healthcare and emergency services. With a presence in 25 countries, our 25,000 skilled professionals delivered more than 9.5 million services last year. We are committed to care, and to care for others, we need to take care of ourselves. That is why we continuously promote engagement, diversity, health, and safety among our employees. Every day, we work to advance sustainable healthcare. And every day, we turn care into action.

About The Team

Our global functions are integral to enabling the organisation to live our purpose. Through dedication and expertise , these teams support Falck in delivering care and making a meaningful difference in people's lives. If you are motivated by contributing to something greater, Falck is the place to bring care into your career .

Title: Souschef - Fuldtid - Jyllinge
Description:
Introduktion

Kan du motivere branchens bedste kolleger og bidrage til, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar? Så er du måske vores nye souschef.

Hos Lidl bliver du en del af Europas største dagligvarekæde med 150+ butikker i Danmark. Sammen er vi drevet af et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, og på #teamlidl kommer du med på en succesfuld rejse –– på et hold med høje ambitioner til fælles. Vi har brug for stærke ledere, der giver vores medarbejdere retning og overskud, så vi sammen skaber rammerne for landets bedste indkøbsoplevelse.

Som souschef tager du aktiv del i det daglige arbejde i butikken, og får medansvar for butikkens drift og resultater. Hos os får du ansvar lige fra første arbejdsdag og bliver samtidig en del af et ambitiøst karriereudviklingsforløb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi nemlig høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig.

Som souschef i Lidl får du en ansvarsfuld rolle med spændende udfordringer og en konkurrencedygtig lønpakke. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, så du har forudsætningerne for at opnå succes i din nye stilling.

Om jobbet

Som souschef er du med til at drive butikken og lede medarbejderne. Du har medansvar for, at butikken lever op til kundernes forventninger, så de bliver ved med at handle hos os. Ud over at bestille varer, fylde op og ekspedere kunderne ved kassen, får du også følgende opgaver, som du løser i tæt samarbejde med butikschefen:


Planlægning af ressourcer og prioritering af arbejdsopgaver
Oplæring af medarbejdere, så de efterlever Lidls værdier og butikskoncepter
Ledelse af medarbejdere så de lykkes i deres job
Sørge for, at vores butikskoncepter overholdes
Ansvar for butikken når butikschefen ikke er til stede


Om dig

Du er en empatisk og stærk ledertype, der har nemt ved motivere dine medarbejdere. Du bliver respekteret for din faglighed, og du sætter en ære i at tage del i arbejdsopgaverne i den daglige drift, og være med til at sikre, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar.

Du er godt rustet til jobbet, hvis du:


Har brancheerfaring og gerne erfaring fra lignende stilling
Har gennemført et lederkursus eller er motiveret for at udvikle dig som leder
Har en venlig og professionel fremtoning
Inspirerer dine medarbejdere til at følge dit gode eksempel
Har overblik og fokus på detaljen
Ser butikken som et team og ikke er bleg for at give en hånd med, når en kollega har brug for hjælp
Har god forståelse for arbejdsprocesser, procedurer og koncepter


Vi tilbyder

Som souschef i Lidl får du:


En udfordrende rolle, hvor tingene går stærkt
Verdensklasse sammenhold
En konkurrencedygtig lønpakke på 26.000 – 32.500 kr. brutto om måneden
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst med HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.


Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Sådan søger du stillingen:


Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV


Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidloest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0635, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.

Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

Title: Er du klinisk neuropsykolog?
Description:
Vil du være med til at udvikle demensudredning på Midt- og Vestsjællands Hospital i Slagelse, har vi en stilling til dig.

Vi har 6 neuropsykologstillinger i afdelingen fordelt på neurologisk afdeling og ambulatorium i Slagelse og på Center for Neurorehabilitering i Næstved. Neuropsykologerne har et tæt fagligt fællesskab på tværs af matriklerne og har fælles ekstern supervision ved en specialist i neuropsykologi.

Dine arbejdsopgaver vil blive sammensat efter dine kompetencer, og du kommer til at:


Gennemføre neuropsykologiske udredninger i både Hukommelsesklinikken og i neurologisk ambulatorium.
Indgå i et stærkt tværfagligt team i Hukommelsesklinikken
Være en del af teamet i neurologisk ambulatorium


Vi har en gruppe af neuropsykologer, hvor udredning og tilrettelæggelse af indsatser er helt centralt.

Vi håber, at du er specialist i klinisk neuropsykologi, men hvis du er på vej, vil vi gerne understøtte din uddannelse med relevante kurser og den nødvendige supervision.

Om Medicin 3

Medicin 3 og Afdeling for Fysio- og Ergoterapi dækker både geriatri, neurologi og neurorehabilitering på specialiseret niveau samt fysio- og ergoterapi på hele sygehuset.

Neurologisk afdeling, Slagelse

Neurologisk Afdeling er beliggende i Slagelse med et sengeafsnit med plads til 20 apopleksipatienter til fase 2 rehabilitering. Disse patienter kommer tidligt til rehabilitering efter akut apopleksi – typisk 2-3 dage efter apopleksi.

Neurologisk ambulatorium dækker hovedområderne inden for neurologi på hovedfunktionsniveau med bl.a. epilepsi, multipel sklerose, Parkinsons sygdom og hjernekræft.

Hukommelsesklinikken er en tværfaglig klinik med neurologer, geriatere og psykiater. Her er neuropsykologen en vigtig partner i den tværfaglige tilgang til patienterne og de pårørende, og du vil komme til at deltage i de tværfaglige konferencer.

Center for Neurorehabilitering, Næstved

Center for Neurorehabilitering, i daglig tale CNN, er placeret på Næstved Sygehus og er Region Sjællands specialafdeling med regionsfunktion for rehabilitering af voksne patienter med følger efter hjerneskade forårsaget af bl.a. ulykke (traume), hjerneblødning og blodprop. Der er i alt 23 senge fordelt på to etager. Et ambulatorium med tilknytning til CNN er netop startet. Alle patienter vurderes tværfagligt under indlæggelsen, som typisk varer 4-6 uger. Neuropsykologen er her en vigtig tovholder i hele forløbet.

Du skal søge stillingen, hvis du er


cand.psyk. med interesse for klinisk neuropsykologi eller hvis du har erfaring med neuropsykologiske undersøgelser
interesseret i at indgå i et bredt tværfagligt miljø
klar til at tage nyt ansvar
interesseret i den hjerneskadede patient og de pårørende
interesseret i demensudredning
autorisation er en fordel, men ikke et krav


Vi tilbyder dig


et neurologisk fagligt stærkt miljø
tilbud om relevante kurser
mulighed for supervision - både intern og ekstern
et godt, tværfagligt miljø på både Næstved og Slagelse Sygehuse


Der tilbydes løn- og ansættelsesvilkår i henhold til overenskomst mellem Danske Regioner og Dansk Psykolog Forening.

Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen kan du kontakte cheflæge Benedikte Wanscher, mail: bewa@regionsjaelland.dk, mobil 93 57 62 51

Ansøgningsfrist: 12/6-2026

Samtaler afholdes 22/6 (eftermiddag)

Om Midt- og Vestsjællands Hospital

På Midt- og Vestsjællands Hospital arbejder vi hver dag for at fremme det sunde liv ved at forebygge og behandle i en sammenhængende sundhedsindsats. Som hospital forener vi psykiatri og somatik. Vores mål er, at patienter og borgere oplever gode og trygge forløb af høj kvalitet.

Som medarbejder bliver du en del af et nytænkende, fagligt og organisatorisk robust hospital, hvor vi gør en forskel for de mennesker, der har brug for os. Læring og udvikling er en del af hverdagen og sker i fagligt forpligtende fællesskaber. Også på tværs af afdelinger.

Midt- og Vestsjællands Hospital dækker et opland på ca. 230.000 borgere. Vi har 766 sengepladser og er omkring 5.000 ansatte fordelt på 20 afdelinger, herunder 16 somatiske og psykiatriske specialer.

Vi tilbyder et solidt forskningsmiljø med 13 professorer og et stærkt fagligt uddannelsesmiljø, hvor vi hvert år byder cirka 2.700 studerende, elever og læger i videreuddannelsesforløb velkommen.

Title: IT-supportkonsulent
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Er du klar til at tage næste skridt i din IT-karriere i en virksomhed i vækst?

Hos LOCA søger vi en struktureret og nysgerrig IT-supportkonsulent. Vi søger en Medarbejder med et par års erfaring, som trives med at arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem IT, data og forretning.

Du får en central rolle i at sikre, udvikle og optimere vores IT-systemer – og bliver en vigtig sparringspartner for både drift, marketing og indkøb.




Dette er ikke en snæver specialistrolle. Det er en mulighed for at få bredt ansvar og opbygge en stærk forretningsforståelse i en organisation, hvor tempoet er højt, og beslutningsvejene er korte.




Din rolle hos os

Du får en alsidig hverdag med både drift, udvikling og analyse – og en rolle hvor du er med til at gøre organisationen mere effektiv og datadrevet.

Dine primære ansvarsområder bliver:

Administration af Sharepoint, IT-licenser og hardware
Superbruger og sparringspartner på vores IT-værktøjer til drift af vores restauranter og kantiner herunder, men ikke alene systemerne:
Online POS, Ajour, Planday, Continia, Plecto, Easy Table
Erfaring i wordpress med udvikling, drift og vedligehold af domæner, hjemmesider og hosting
Teknisk support til marketing, bl.a. ift. hjemmesideopbygning og systemer til sociale medier 
Implementering og optimering af IT-systemer, herunder muligheder med AI
Support til kolleger i restauranter og kantiner
Analyse af indkøb og optimering af processer
Bidrag til udbud, benchmarking og leverandørvalg
Identificere effektiviseringer og omkostningsbesparelser
Samling af KPI’er og BI i én rapportering




Hvem er du?

Vi forestiller os, at du:

Har erfaring fra en lignende stilling med IT-drift og IT-værktøjer
Kan arbejde hands-on med f.eks. WordPress og de nævnte IT-værktøjer.
Har erfaring med Meta, LinkedIn og Google.
Arbejder struktureret og er komfortabel med data og analyser

Et fuldtidsjob i København.

Du får en central rolle i at sikre, udvikle og optimere vores IT-systemer – og bliver en vigtig sparringspartner for både drift, marketing og indkøb.


Dette er ikke en snæver specialistrolle. Det er en mulighed for at få bredt ansvar og opbygge en stærk forretningsforståelse i en organisation, hvor tempoet er højt, og beslutningsvejene er korte.


Din rolle hos os

Du får en alsidig hverdag med både drift, udvikling og analyse – og en rolle hvor du er med til at gøre organisationen mere effektiv og datadrevet.

Title: Associate Analytics Lead - BI
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Finance, Legal & Global Solutions

Bagsvaerd, Denmark

There has never been a better time to join indirect procurement at Novo Nordisk. We are driving a global cost-optimisation programme where analytics is a key enabler—converting complex data into clear insights and real business impact. If you want to build high-impact BI, elevate source-to-pay and category excellence, and succeed in a fast-moving, stakeholder-rich environment, this role is an exciting opportunity.

Your new role

As an Associate Analytics Lead – BI, you will be part of the Advanced Analytics team and work in close collaboration with experienced data and BI professionals, procurement system, process specialists and category managers to generate meaningful insights from large and complex data sources. You will be responsible for turning data into actionable, timely, and impactful insights, enabling high-quality analytics and dashboards that support strategic procurement decisions and drive value creation.

Key responsibilities


Act as a trusted analytics and BI partner within Corporate Procurement, driving the development, rollout, and continuous improvement of Power BI dashboards, analytics products, and performance management.
Define, standardize, and govern procurement KPIs, ensuring transparent definitions, consistent measurement, and clear data lineage from source systems to dashboards.
Provide actionable insights across the end-to-end Source-to-Pay area to identify opportunities for process improvement, efficiency gains and user experience enhancement.
Support category management analytics, including spend and savings analysis, market intelligence, and preferred supplier usage, helping category managers drive value creation.


Your new department

Corporate Procurement is the Centre of Excellence for procurement in Novo Nordisk. We are responsible for the global procurement strategy, processes, and systems. We define and drive the global procurement strategy with around 150 employees in Denmark and over 500 globally, acting as a strategic partner in executing the company’s strategy and enabling value at scale. A key enabler for taking Procurement to the next level will be to leverage holistic insights to drive value in and from procurement. The Advanced Analytics team is responsible for driving this journey.

Your Skills And Qualifications


Bachelor/Master’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, Computer Science, or a similar field.
5–7+ years of experience in BI and analytics or related fields. Experience in Procurement BI is a plus.
Extensive hands-on experience with Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, Power Query, semantic models, dashboard design, report optimisation, and deployment of scalable BI solutions. Understanding of modern BI and data platforms.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric, Azure Databricks is an advantage. You are curious, think outside the box, and are eager to explore how AI can transform procurement from passive reporting to proactive analytics that accelerate decision-making and enhance user experience.
You are structured in your problem-solving approach and able to break down complex business questions into clear analytical tasks. You are a strong team member with a can-do attitude and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
You can navigate a complex stakeholder landscape and communicate effectively with stakeholders from different functions, seniority levels, cultures, and domain backgrounds.


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.

Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 651,000 to 956,900 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here

Deadline

26 May, 2026

To ensure a fair and efficient recruitment process, please refrain from adding a photo in your CV or resume.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you be-come part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.
