Title: Data Engineer - Team Data Foundation
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

What's in it for you?

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.

International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

Who are you ideally?


You think of data not just as pipelines, but as a product. You care about who consumes the data - whether it's a customer running queries in their workspace or an internal team building on top of your platform - and you design with their experience in mind.
You bring a strong background in data engineering and are proficient in Python, SQL, and Spark. From streaming ingestion to historical backfills, from performance tuning to choosing the right partitioning strategy - these are the kinds of challenges that motivate you.
You have hands-on experience with Databricks and Delta Lake, or similar lakehouse technologies.
You understand the medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) and know how to model data for different consumption patterns.
You're curious about how AI and large language models can be enabled on top of large-scale data - and you're excited to help build the foundation that makes that possible.
Experience with Kafka, multi-tenant data architectures, or IoT/telematics data is a strong plus.


Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Primary Responsibilities

Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.

Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.

Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.

Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.

Our hiring process

Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.

Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team.

Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.

If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.

Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

Coming Together To Connect Construction

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.

The question is: Are you in?

Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Primary Responsibilities

Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.

Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.

Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.

Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.

Our hiring process

Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.

Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team.

Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.

If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.

Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

Coming Together To Connect Construction

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.

The question is: Are you in?

HTML:
Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

What's in it for you?

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.

International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

Title: Opvågningssygeplejerske
Place: Hillerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv en del af udviklingen i opvågningsafsnittet, og få en unik mulighed for at arbejde med både korte, intense patientforløb og mere komplicerede patientforløb

I vores dynamiske og tværfaglige team, har vi fokus på tæt samarbejde for at sikre høj kvalitet og det bedst mulige patientforløb.

Hos Os Får Du


en alsidig hverdag med både komplekse og sammedagskirurgiske patientforløb
et afsnit hvor vores specialeansvarlige sygeplejersker medvirker til et højt fagligt niveau
onboardingprogram med mentorordning tilpasset dine kompetencer og behov
personlig og faglig kompetenceudvikling med kompetencekort og formaliseret efteruddannelse
mulighed for weekendvagt hver 4. weekend og medindflydelse på egen vagtplan


Vi Har Brug For Dig, Der


har dansk autorisation som sygeplejerske
gerne har erfaring fra opvågning, sammedagskirurgi eller kirurgiske specialer
er faglig engageret, trives i en afdeling der er i hastig udvikling frem mod det nye hospital
evner at arbejde selvstændigt, tage initiativ og inspirere kolleger.


Vi er ca. 30 sygeplejersker i et velfungerende afsnit med fokus på kollegial omsorg/sparring og har en kultur der bevidst arbejder med samarbejde og trivsel.

Vi værdsætter sammenhæng mellem arbejdsliv og familieliv.

Vi har daglig synlig ledelse med højt informationsniveau og åben dialog.

Bliv en del af et afsnit med mangeartede og varierede patientforløb

Opvågningsafsnittet har døgnåbent og modtager patienter i alle aldre fra samtlige af hospitalets specialer.

Afsnittet har en kapacitet på 21 senge fordelt på det centrale afsnit og et satellit-afsnit til øre- næse- og halspatienter. Begge afsnit modtager både akutte og elektive sammedags kirurgiske patienter samt indlagte patienter med mere komplicerede forløb.

Endvidere modtages patienter til anlæggelse af centrale venekatetre, epidurale katetre, bloodpatch, perifere nerveblokader og patienter til præoperativ optimering.

Afsnittes sygeplejersker varetager den postoperative epidurale smertebehandling på hele hospitalet.

Vi har et tæt samarbejde med det operative område og de kirurgiske specialer.

Vi deltager i kvalitets- og forskningsprojekter.

Afsnittet fungerer som praktiksted for sygeplejestuderende, ambulancebehandler elever samt kursister fra specialuddannelserne til intensiv- og anæstesisygeplejerske.

En spændende udvikling frem mod det nye hospital, som forventes færdigt i 2027

Vi er i gang med en omfattende udvikling, hvor vores fire opvågningsafsnit samles til ét stort fælles afsnit, som skal håndtere både sammedagskirurgiske, elektive og akutte patienter. Samtidig får det traditionelle opvågningsafsnit en udvidet funktion, hvor vi også varetager hele sammedagskirurgiske forløb – fra modtagelse til udskrivelse.

Denne udvikling er en del af forberedelserne til Nyt Hospital Nordsjælland (NHN), hvor det sammedagskirurgiske område udvides markant. Allerede nu er der etableret en fælles afsnitsledelse for opvågning og dagkirurgi, som med tiden fusioneres til ét samlet afsnit, der modtager al elektiv og akut kirurgi.

Forandringen medfører nye opgaver og samarbejdsformer, som styrker både fleksibilitet og faglighed. Vi har fokus på effektivt patientflow, høj faglig kvalitet, patientsikkerhed og patienttilfredshed.

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig

Stillingen er baseret på 37 timer eller efter aftale.

Vi arbejder i skiftende vagter, og har forskellige mødetider.

Stillingen er til besættelse 01.08.26 eller efter aftale.

Ansøgning og CV sendes via linket her på siden.

Ansøgningsfrist er den d. 09.06.26. Vi afholder samtaler den 17/6.

Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår følger gældende overenskomst.

Autoriseret sundhedspersonale med patientkontakt, der ikke har en dansk uddannelse, skal ved ansættelse i Region Hovedstaden have bestået en danskprøve (Danskuddannelse 3, som svarer til 9. klasses afgangseksamen) eller som minimum kunne bestå prøven senest 1 år efter ansættelsen, eller have opnået tilsvarende sprogkompetencer i dansk på anden vis.

Er du blevet nysgerrig, og vil du vide mere?

Hvis du vil vide mere om eller ønsker et uforpligtigende besøg i afsnittet, er du velkommen til at kontakte oversygeplejerske Lene Höegh på telefon 4829 5865.

Læs mere om afsnittet her

Title: Deltid - Salgsassistent
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hos Envii leder vi efter en deltidsmedarbejder, der kan spejle sig i vores univers og måde at arbejde på. Har du en passion for mode, og har du lyst til at kickstarte din karriere i en voksende skandinavisk modeorganisation? Hvis du drømmer om at blive en del af et fællesskab, hvor hverdagen er fyldt med god energi og kreativitet, så er denne stilling i vores butik på Frederiksberggade måske noget for dig!

STILLINGEN

Som salgsassistent hos Envii bliver du en vigtig del af vores team og er med til at vise, hvem vi er, og hvad vi står for. Din hverdag kommer især til at handle om at hjælpe vores kunder og sikre, at de altid går fra butikken med en god oplevelse. Derudover hjælper du med opfyldning af varer og bidrager med dit gode humør, så butikken føles som et rart og indbydende sted at være.

ARBEJDSOPGAVER


Hjælpe vores kunder med at finde de varer, der passer bedst til deres stil og behov
Bidrage til at nå butikkens salgsmål ved at give kunderne en fremragende service
Skabe gode relationer og interaktioner med vores kunder
Holde butikken pæn, ryddelig og indbydende
Hjælpe med udpakning og opfyldning af varer
Holde dig opdateret om Enviis univers, styles og produktmaterialer


DIN PROFIL


Du møder kunderne med et smil og tager initiativ til at skabe en god stemning omkring dig
Du er serviceminded og har en passion for at sælge
Du har interesse for mode og personlig styling
Du kan arbejde selvstændigt og tage beslutninger, når der er brug for det
Du er fleksibel mht. arbejdstider og er mødestabil
Du er en teamplayer og trives sammen med andre mennesker


Lyder denne stilling som noget for dig, og kan du se dig selv som en del af vores team? Så vil vi elske at høre fra dig.

Vi er engagerede i at skabe et mangfoldigt og inkluderende miljø og opfordrer alle til at søge stillingen, uanset alder, national og kulturel oprindelse, kønsidentitet, religion, seksuel orientering eller handicap.

Vores historie

Envii-historien begyndte i 2009 med vores ønske om at fuldende garderoben for skandinaviske kvinder. Siden da har vi præsenteret prisvenlig mode til hverdag og enhver anledning uden at gå på kompromis med kvaliteten.

Vi blev grundlagt og har base i København, hvor vores flagship-butik åbnede i 2013. I dag har vi etableret os som et go-to brand for moderne kvinder med 7 selvstændige butikker og 8 shop-in-shops i Danmark.

Vores mission er at skabe et inspirerende modeunivers ved at kombinere langtidsholdbare favoritter med sæsonens trends. Vi tror på, at vi på den måde kan motivere alle til selvsikkert at udtrykke deres individualitet og fejre sig selv gennem den måde, de klæder sig på.

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
The PureGym Way

The PureGym Group is a global gym business with a community of more than 700 gyms and over 2 million members. At PureGym, everything we do comes back to one simple idea: helping you feel good.

We're proud of our people and have a strong focus on internal progression. Championing diversity, we are committed to providing an excellent employee experience and workplace culture. As such, we are a Disability Confident Committed employer, and offer interviews to candidates who meet the essential criteria for a role, and opt-in to the scheme on their application form.

Our gyms are friendly, supportive, and judgement-free spaces where everybody can come in, work out and leave Feeling PureGym Good. We are proud to be certified by Top Employers Institute. See our careers page for full benefits.




We offer

Free nationwide gym membership for you + 1
Hybrid working
A truly flexible working culture
Personal private healthcare, including digital GP
Life insurance x4
Company pension contribution
25 days annual leave, plus 1 personal day
Option to purchase additional holiday (up to 5 days)
Great learning & development resources
Enhanced maternity pay, paternity and adoption leave




Location: Hybrid- London (3 days in the office)

Type: Full Time

Contract type: Permanent

Application: Closing date 22nd May 2026

We are a value business, focused on making gyms and fitness accessible to as many people as possible. Data is at the heart of how we run our business, starting from where to open our gyms, through what we offer within them, to how we engage with past, current, and future members. Combining the data from our 2.5+ million members with data about locations, operations, and performance allows us to unlock industry leading insights and drive a better member experience and commercial performance.

As an Insights Analyst you will be at the heart of solving some of the hardest data challenges in the business, turning data into insights and business value through a combination of in-depth analysis and reporting creation. You will be leveraging one of the biggest fitness data assets in the world, and working with state-of-the-art technologies, to help determine what drives member behaviour and commercial outcomes across all our operating geographies.




The Role

As a small, agile team, Insights works closely with its stakeholders, putting data at the heart of decision making across the business. While reporting to the Insights Manager, in this role you get to work closely with stakeholders at all levels across the business. You will also work in close collaboration with the wider Insights & Analytics functions, which provides ample opportunity to learn from a group of talented and experienced Data Scientists and Data Engineers.

In this role, you will have the opportunity to:

Work on business-critical analytics projects and reporting, identifying outcomes and communicating findings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business - Commercial, Finance, Operational and Marketing teams, in addition to Data Science and Data Engineering.
Build and maintain the central data product serving KPIs and insights to the entire organisation.
Work with Azure Databricks, PowerBI and dbt technology, leveraging modern cloud-native technologies and data lakehouse architecture.
Drive continuous improvement to how we enable data driven decision making in PureGym.




The Person

We would expect some or all of the following:

Experience in data analytics / insights
Proficient with SQL, Power BI, Excel and PowerPoint
Additionally, experience with dbt would be an advantage
A drive to turn insights into compelling stories for stakeholders to understand, for them to make more effective, data-led decisions
Strong commercial awareness with an interest in B2C

We are looking for an ambitious individual with a passion for leveraging analytics to drive business outcomes. The following core attributes are essential:

Excellent problem-solving: someone who enjoys applying their mind to many and varied business problems and be a driving force behind solving them
Innovative and curious: constantly seek to learn and find new and better ways of doing things; not just solving problems but seeking them out; always interested to find new and better methods and technologies
Biased towards action: take the initiative and get things done under their own steam and in collaboration with others
Collaborative: driven to engage with colleagues from across the business and support and enable your team members
A great communicator: able to translate complex concepts and analyses into digestible recommendations that can be easily interpreted and acted upon




PureGym is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. Our company mantra is ‘Everybody Welcome’ and we are dedicated to promoting a diverse and inclusive place to work. From a hiring standpoint, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to ensuring that our PureGym colleagues reflect the diversity of the nation as well as our millions of gym members we serve.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and the advert may be withdrawn at any time. Early application is encouraged.

We're proud of our people and have a strong focus on internal progression. Championing diversity, we are committed to providing an excellent employee experience and workplace culture. We offer

  • Free nationwide gym membership for you + 1
  • Hybrid working
  • A truly flexible working culture
  • Personal private healthcare, including digital GP

  • Company pension contribution
  • 25 days annual leave, plus 1 personal day
  • Option to purchase additional holiday (up to 5 days)

Type: Full Time

We are a value business, focused on making gyms and fitness accessible to as many people as possible. Data is at the heart of how we run our business, starting from where to open our gyms, through what we offer within them, to how we engage with past, current, and future members. Combining the data from our 2.5+ million members with data about locations, operations, and performance allows us to unlock industry leading insights and drive a better member experience and commercial performance.

As an Insights Analyst you will be at the heart of solving some of the hardest data challenges in the business, turning data into insights and business value through a combination of in-depth analysis and reporting creation. You will be leveraging one of the biggest fitness data assets in the world, and working with state-of-the-art technologies, to help determine what drives member behaviour and commercial outcomes across all our operating geographies.


The Role

As a small, agile team, Insights works closely with its stakeholders, putting data at the heart of decision making across the business. Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Who are we?👋

Look at the latest headlines and you will see something Ki insures. Think space shuttles, world tours, wind farms, and even footballers' legs. 


Ki's mission is simple. Digitally disrupt and revolutionise a 335-year-old market. Working with Google and UCL, Ki has created a platform that uses algorithms, machine learning and large language models to give insurance brokers quotes in seconds, rather than days. 


Ki is proudly the biggest global algorithmic insurance carrier. It is the fastest growing syndicate in the Lloyd's of London market, and the first ever to make $100m in profit in 3 years. 


Ki's teams have varied backgrounds and work together in an agile, cross-functional way to build the very best experience for its customers. Ki has big ambitions but needs more excellent minds to challenge the status-quo and help it reach new horizons.


Where you come in?

The Data Analyst at Ki works at the intersection of analytics, engineering and product. They work within teams and across Ki's teams to support and drive design, development, and optimization of analytics, data models, reports, and other data-driven products that support strategic business goals.  


What you will be doing: 🖋️


Applied Analytics

Develop knowledge and expertise in Ki's business domains to generate insights and measurement on initiatives that drive company objectives.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify opportunities for leveraging data analytics to drive commercial results and improve operational efficiency.
Identify opportunities to leverage data analytics techniques to extract value from both internal and external data assets to enrich analytical capabilities.


Analytics Engineering

Develop and maintain the core data sets underlying the suite of analytical tools used by the team
Create and maintain data visualizations and dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Develop scalable data solutions that conform to Ki's technology and engineering principles and industry best practice.


Data Management & Governance

Preserve the integrity of data, making sure it is accurate, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle.
Investigate and lead timely resolution of emerging issues with underlying data systems and models as and when these arise.
Ensure that domain data is managed and used according to Ki's data governance policy and best practice principles.


Other

Ensure all data systems and models are documented in line with Ki wide standards
Recommend ways to improve data efficiency and reliability
Increase the degree of automation within the team's data systems and tools
Investigate new tools that would help the team store, structure and analyse data.


Requirements


Proficiency in SQL and Python to manipulate and analyse datasets 
Experience with data reporting and visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Dash, Streamlit) 
Solid understanding of statistical techniques (e.g. regression, clustering, correlation analysis), with ability to apply these to commercial problem solving. 
Experience working in cloud native environments (especially GCP) would be plus. 
Experience with designing and implementation of data structures to ensure ease of use and accessibility by a broader audience. 
Self-organised, good at documenting and communicating findings and approach. 
Excellent communicator, able to present complex analysis to both technical and non-technical audiences. 
Experience working in a regulated industry


What to expect during the recruitment process:

Initial recruiter screening call
Interview with hiring manager
Technical Interview (this may vary depending on the role)
Values Interview

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Up to £50 per hour Inside IR35

Filton, UK (Hybrid)

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our client on a contract basis, supporting critical aerospace projects. This is an exceptional opportunity to contribute to innovative initiatives within the aerospace industry, renowned for its technology-driven environment and commitment to excellence.

The successful candidate will play a vital part in shaping data-driven decision-making processes that impact aircraft safety, efficiency, and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse aircraft health data utilizing Skywise and Python tools to identify trends and insights.
Collaborate with Systems Engineering teams to interpret and enhance aircraft system performance.
Support health engineering activities related to aircraft lifecycle management.
Use in-service data to improve in-service operations understanding and system design.
Contribute to the development of fleet diagnostic and predictive maintenance models.
Support technical investigations with aircraft fleet data analysis.

Essential Skills & Experience

Proven experience in data analysis within the aerospace sector or similar high-technology environment.
Proficiency in Python programming for data manipulation and analysis.
Practical knowledge of Skywise or comparable aviation data platforms.
Strong understanding of aircraft systems and health engineering principles.
Ability to interpret complex data sets into actionable insights.
Experience with Jira, Palantir Foundry (Skywise), Extended Platform (Jupyter), Github.
Data Analytics & Data Visualisation skills.
Good stakeholder management skills.

Desirable Skills & Experience

Knowledge of Skywise Predictive Maintenance (SPM).
LG System understanding.
Experience with Manage Fleet Diagnostic and Predictive Maintenance process (SU.CP.05).
Previous experience in Predictive Maintenance and ZAOG plateau.
Familiarity with AI and Machine Learning techniques.
Highly organised, delivery-oriented, and capable of working independently.

If you are looking to apply data analytics within a dynamic aerospace setting and are eager to make a tangible impact, we invite you to submit your CV for consideration. This role offers the chance to work on innovative projects in a forward-thinking organisation committed to technological advancement and professional development.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
At Warner Music Group, We’re a Global Collective Of Music Makers And Music Lovers, Tech Innovators And Inspired Entrepreneurs, Game-changing Creatives And Passionate Team Members. Here, We Turn Dreams Into Stardom And Audiences Into Fans. We Are Guided By Three Core Values That Underpin Everything We Do Across All Our Diverse Businesses


Curiosity: We do our best work when we’re immersing ourselves in culture and breaking through barriers. Curiosity is the driving force behind creativity and ingenuity. It fuels innovation, and innovation is the key to our future.
Collaboration: Making music and bringing it to the world is all about the power of originality amplified by teamwork. A great idea, like a great song, travels globally. We ignite passions and build connections across our diverse community of artists, songwriters, partners, and fans.
Commitment: We pursue excellence for our team and our talent. Everything in music starts with a leap into the unknown, and we’re committed to keeping the faith, acting with integrity, and delivering on our promises.


WMG is home to a wide range of artists, musicians, and songwriters that fuel our success. That is why we are committed to creating a work environment that actively values, appreciates, and respects everyone. We encourage applications from people with a wide variety of backgrounds and experiences.

Consider a career at WMG and get the best of both worlds – an innovative global music company that retains the creative spirit of a nimble independent.

Job Title: Junior Data Analyst

A Little Bit About Our Team

As part of Warner Music Group, ADA gives independent labels and artists access to a global distribution system and a dedicated team that provides all aspects of marketing and promotion services. ADA enjoys the benefit of inclusion in all of WMG's revenue assurance services as well as its company-wide deals. 

Your Role

You will report to the Director of Streaming & Commercial Strategy, supporting the delivery of streaming, audience and commercial insights across a diverse roster of artists and genres. This is a fast-paced, highly collaborative role suited to someone who is both analytical and commercially curious, with a passion for music, data and digital culture.

You will play a key role in helping the business understand artist performance, audience behaviour and market trends, translating large datasets into clear, actionable insights that support campaign strategy and business growth. The role will also contribute to the development of AI-driven tools, reporting efficiencies and internal data processes.

Here You’ll Get To


Deliver bespoke reporting, analysis and insights across artist campaigns, streaming platforms and market trends.
Support strategic discussions by communicating key artist, audience and commercial insights to the teams.
Work with large datasets using SQL/Snowflake and Tableau (or similar BI tools) to produce and analyse daily, weekly and campaign-level performance reporting.
Monitor UK market trends, streaming consumption and DSP partner performance.
Partner with A&R teams to provide data-led insight on potential signings and emerging talent opportunities.
Assist in the development of internal tools, automations and reporting processes to improve team efficiency and scalability.
Build audience and consumption models to help inform audience growth and acquisition strategies.
Identify patterns, trends and opportunities across campaigns, genres and the wider streaming landscape.
Explore and support the implementation of AI and LLM-based solutions to enhance reporting, insight generation and operational efficiency.


About You


Highly analytical with strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Able to work independently and handle multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Comfortable interpreting large datasets and translating complex findings into clear, actionable insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to tailor insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience writing SQL queries and working with large datasets.
Experience building dashboards and visualisations in Tableau or a similar BI platform.
Strong understanding of how statistical and analytical techniques can be applied to business and audience data.
Familiarity with AI tools, LLMs and workflow automation concepts.
Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Collaborative approach with the ability to work effectively across teams in a matrix-style organisation.
Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills and confidence working with complex formulas and datasets.


We’d Love It If You Also Had


Previous experience in an analyst, data or insights role within music, media or entertainment.
Familiarity with Python or other programming languages used for analytics and automation.
Knowledge of music industry analytics platforms such as Chartmetric, Luminate, Soundcharts or similar tools.
Understanding of streaming ecosystem dynamics, audience development and DSP best practices.
Experience using Airtable, Google Workspace and Microsoft Office tools.
Passion for music, digital culture and emerging technology.


As the home to 10K Projects, Asylum, Atlantic Music Group, East West, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics, and several other of the world’s premier recording labels, Warner Music Group champions emerging artists and global superstars alike. And our renowned publishing company, Warner Chappell Music, represents genre-spanning songwriters and producers through a catalog of more than one million copyrights worldwide. Warner Music Group is also home to ADA, which supports the independent community, as well as artist services division WMX. In addition, WMG counts film and television storytelling powerhouse Warner Music Entertainment among its many brands.

Together, we are Warner Music Group: Independent Minds. Major Sound.

Love this job and want to apply?

Click the “Apply” link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. Applying with LinkedIn will import all of the information you put in your profile, but will still allow you to upload a resume and cover letter.

Don’t be discouraged if you don’t hear from us right away. We’re taking our time to review all resumes, and to find the best people for WMG.

Thanks for your interest in working for WMG. We love it here, and think you will, too.

This position requires a minimum of 4 days per week in the office. We value in-office collaboration, which is essential for talent development and strong working relationships.

WMG is committed to inclusion and diversity in all aspects of our business. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and will evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, religion or belief, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or gender reassignment, marital or civil partnership status, disability, pregnancy, childbirth or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




📍 London (3 days a week) | 6 Month Contract




We’re looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join a high-performing Data & Insights team within a fast-paced, commercial environment.

This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in delivering insights that directly drive revenue, improve performance, and shape business strategy.




The Role

As a Data Analyst, you’ll partner with teams across Marketing, Sales, Finance, Content and Operations to deliver high-quality insights that enable smarter decision-making and tangible business impact.

You’ll work with large, complex datasets to uncover trends, identify opportunities, and ensure data remains a strategic asset across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities

Deliver data analysis, reporting, and insight requests across multiple business functions
Analyse and interpret data from a variety of sources to inform strategy and performance
Manage and maintain master data and mapping tables (companies, industries, products)
Generate actionable insights and communicate findings clearly to stakeholders
Support revenue optimisation (delegate acquisition, retention, sponsorship, digital products)
Champion data quality and collaborate with data teams to improve processes
Assist in building dashboards and self-service reporting tools

About You

5+ years’ experience in data analysis within a fast-paced, commercial environment
Strong experience working with large, complex datasets (cleaning, manipulation, analysis)
Confident working with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs
Experience with CRM systems (e.g. Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics)
Advanced Excel / Google Sheets skills
Strong attention to detail and organisational skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines effectively

Desirable:

SQL / relational database knowledge
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker)

Why Join?

High-impact role influencing commercial performance
Collaborative, data-driven team environment
Opportunity to work across multiple business functions
Fast-paced and dynamic setting




If you’re a data-driven problem solver who thrives on turning insights into action, we’d love to hear from you.

👉 Apply now or reach out for more information.

    • Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker)

    • High-impact role influencing commercial performance
    • Collaborative, data-driven team environment
    • Opportunity to work across multiple business functions
    • Fast-paced and dynamic setting


    If you’re a data-driven problem solver who thrives on turning insights into action, we’d love to hear from you.

    • 👉 Apply now or reach out for more information.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Industry: Designer toys and collectible pieces

Location: London, Canary Wharf

Job type: Full time Permanent




About POP MART




POP MART (09992.HK) is a leading global company in the trend culture and entertainment industry. Centered around IP, POP MART has built a comprehensive platform for creative incubation and IP operation, empowering global creators while delivering exciting products, services and immersive entertainment experiences to consumers.




POP MART identifies and nurtures emerging artists and designers worldwide, creating popular character IPs through a well-established development and operation system. Our portfolio includes iconic characters such as MOLLY, SKULLPANDA, DIMOO, THE MONSTERS and Hirono. POP MART operates over 500 physical stores and more than 2,300 Robo Shops across more than thirty countries and regions, and through multiple cross border e commerce platforms reaches audiences in over ninety countries and regions, bringing joy to young consumers around the world.




POP MART has developed a comprehensive IP commercialisation ecosystem that spans multiple industries, deepening emotional bonds between fans and IPs while exploring broader opportunities for value creation. Our ventures include the world’s first immersive intellectual property themed park in the art toy industry, the expansion of its IPs into global markets through licensing and co-branding with leading global brands, and the curation of art toy expos and intellectual property themed exhibitions internationally. In Europe, POP MART has built a strong presence in key markets including the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands and Spain, and is expanding into new high potential markets. Our expansion brings POP MART closer to European fans and offers new ways for them to connect with our IPs and stories.




For more information, please visit www.popmart.com and our LinkedIn Career page.




What to Expect




POP MART is a fast-paced company with a young and multicultural team where you can expect to:




Contribute to a fast-growing European business that combines retail, art, and creative storytelling
Work with passionate teams across different countries, all connected by a shared love for designer toys and unique customer experiences
Grow your skills through hands-on projects, collaborative learning, and opportunities to explore new ideas




Benefits




POP MART offers our employees:




Monthly appreciation gifts featuring our latest POP MART designs
A structured bonus scheme linked to both company and individual performance
25 days of annual leave, in addition to bank holidays
4x life insurance, together with group medical and travel insurance
A confidential Employee Assistance Programme, alongside access to a discount platform for selected online retailers
Training and development programmes, together with regular team events that encourage learning, collaboration, and celebration




The Role and Candidate




We are looking for a data driven and detail oriented professional to support business performance monitoring and analysis across POP MART’s European business.

This role focuses on operational reporting, performance tracking, and business insight generation across key revenue channels, including physical stores, vending machines, and online business. You will support the business by monitoring core operational indicators, identifying trends and issues through data, and helping teams better understand performance through clear, accurate, and practical analysis.

You will also work closely with teams including Finance, Retail Operations, Marketing, Merchandising, and Business Development to support regular reporting, operational reviews, and day to day data requests. In parallel, the role will contribute to improving reporting consistency, data quality, and the practical use of data in business operations.

This position would suit someone with solid analytical capability, structured thinking, and a hands on approach, who is comfortable working with detailed data while supporting wider business needs in a fast paced environment.




Responsibilities




Daily Operation Monitoring and Performance Review




Establish daily, weekly and monthly monitoring for core operational indicators, including revenue, gross profit, expense ratio, payment collection, labour efficiency and store efficiency, across all revenue channels such as physical stores, vending machines and online business
Prepare daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly operational reports in a timely manner, benchmark performance against budget, year on year and month on month data, and analyse the causes of performance gaps
Track key business indicators including sales volume, average transaction value, conversion rate and profit margin, and provide regular operational analysis to support performance improvement
Analyse the performance of different markets, stores and vending machines, identify trends and issues, and support the development of targeted actions to improve profitability
Support the Business Development team with data input for store and vending machine site selection, based on market data and operational analysis
Support monthly and quarterly operational review meetings by preparing review materials and presentations, highlighting business progress, key issues and improvement priorities, and tracking follow up actions
Work closely with the Finance team to verify operational, cost and expense data, align statistical definitions, resolve data discrepancies, and ensure management reporting is accurate and reliable




Business Insights and Reporting




Work closely with business teams on a daily basis to understand key business pain points, and support practical data solutions based on business needs and operating scenarios
Conduct data analysis across sales operations, marketing and consumer insights, identify the business logic behind the data, and provide insights to support business optimisation
Improve the quality and depth of recurring insight reports by moving beyond data listing and strengthening business focused conclusions and recommendations
Build and maintain structured data records and archives to support data traceability, consistency and reusability
Extract and process data from multiple sources, including business systems, CRM and ERP systems, and use SQL for regular queries, data cleaning, deduplication, correlation and aggregation
Build and maintain basic dashboards and automated reports to reduce manual reporting, improve data visibility, and support more efficient access to information




Cross Functional Collaboration and Data Governance




Support Sales, Marketing, Product, Supply Chain, Finance and other teams with day to day data requests, including customised queries, report exports and basic analysis support
Provide guidance to business teams on basic data viewing and analysis methods to improve the practical use of data in daily operations
Support the standardisation of data fields, statistical rules and analysis templates, and help establish data logs and archiving practices to strengthen data governance
Contribute to the development of a more consistent and practical data culture across the business




Required Qualifications




3–5 years of business analysis experience; candidates with experience in management consulting, internet business analysis, retail operations analysis, or other relevant analytical experience, or those with extensive professional experience are preferred




Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities




Proactive, hands-on, and entrepreneurial mindset with experience working in a fast-paced business environment




Outstanding problem-solving, analytical, written and verbal communication skills




Data-sensitive, able to identify business trends and issues through data analysis; proficiency in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Excel, SQL, Tableau is preferred, but strong willingness to learn is a must




Proficiency in English (listening, speaking, reading and writing), with extensive experience in cross-team project collaboration and communication







Desirable but Not Essential




Experience in the toy industry or pop culture retail sector
Familiarity with Local and European retail landscapes
Understanding of designer toy and collectible trends, particularly in the art toy market
Experience supporting operational reporting across multiple countries or regions
Understanding of retail KPIs including conversion rate, ATV, margin, labour efficiency and inventory performance
Exposure to data governance, reporting automation, or process improvement initiatives
Experience within retail, consumer goods, eCommerce, or multi-site operations environments
Experience working with ERP, CRM, POS or business intelligence systems
Familiarity with dashboard tools such as Tableau, Power BI or Looker










If you are excited to be part of POP MART’s journey and believe you have the skills and passion to contribute, click Apply Now and send us your CV.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Merrifield Consultants is recruiting a Data Analyst for a respected professional body. This role sits in a small data insights team, producing evidence and analysis on areas such as membership, students, teachers, awards, research and the wider science landscape.




This is a fixed term contract until 31 December 2026, ideally starting as soon as possible.




Data Analyst

Salary: Between £34,800 to £36,800, pro rata (depending on experience)

Hours: 35 hours per week

Location: Mostly home based, with monthly team meetings, quarterly meetings and occasional office attendance when needed




The role will involve:

Working with messy data and turning it into clear, usable information
Using Python for data manipulation, analysis and reporting
Using Microsoft tools and AWS for access, storage and workflows
Producing clear visualisations and reports
Keeping KPIs up to date
Analysing internal data, including membership numbers, awards, students and teachers
Supporting general data analysis across projects




We are looking for someone with:

Good data theory and a sound understanding of data structures
Strong data manipulation and reporting experience
Confident Python skills
Experience producing clear visualisations
The ability to explain findings simply to different audiences
A careful, practical approach to data quality and accuracy




This would suit a Data Analyst who is confident with Python, understands how to work with imperfect data, and wants to work on useful evidence-led projects in a research, membership or not-for-profit setting.




For more information or to apply, please contact Akash Mahmud at Merrifield Consultants.




We practice a diverse and inclusive recruitment process that ensures equal opportunity for all we work with, irrespective of race, sexual orientation, mental or physical disability, age or gender. As an organisation, we encourage applications from all backgrounds and will ensure measures are met when required, to allow a fair process throughout.

Title: Data Analysis Intern - London
Description:
Duration: 3 months, with the possibility of progressing into a full-time role

Location: London / Hybrid




Company Description

At HostUplift, we manage short-term rental properties across the UK and internationally — and we’re growing fast. We’re looking for a driven and analytical graduate to join our team as a Data Analysis Intern.




This is a 3-month internship with the possibility of a full-time role at the end, depending on performance and business needs.




This is a brilliant opportunity for someone at the start of their career to gain hands-on experience in data analysis, property performance, pricing strategy, and operational improvement within a fast-growing property management business.




Role Description

You’ll join our close-knit team and work on a key business project focused on analysing property performance and pricing models across our portfolio.




Working alongside experienced colleagues across the business, you’ll help identify where properties are performing well, where improvements can be made, and when pricing changes should be recommended.




Key Responsibiliies

Analyse property performance data across HostUplift’s short-term rental portfolio.
Review pricing models and identify areas where pricing could be improved.
Identify trends, patterns, and performance gaps across different properties, locations, and markets.
Assess when changes should be made to property pricing based on occupancy, revenue, seasonality, market trends, and performance data.
Prepare clear reports and summaries of your findings for the team.
Support the development of dashboards, trackers, and reporting tools.
Help identify opportunities to improve revenue, occupancy, and overall property performance.




About You

A recent graduate from a top-tier UK university with a strong academic background.
Keen to start or grow a career in data analysis.
Strong analytical skills and confidence working with data.
Sharp attention to detail and pride in producing accurate work.
Comfortable using Excel or Google Sheets to analyse and present data.
A quick learner who adapts confidently to new tools, systems, and processes.
A collaborative team player who enjoys working across different functions.




What’s on Offer

A 3-month internship with the possibility of a full-time role at the end.
Hands-on experience working at the heart of a fast-growing, international property management business.
Real exposure to property performance, pricing strategy, revenue optimisation, and data-driven decision-making.
The opportunity to work on a meaningful project with direct business impact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Airtime is a leading data-driven rewards platform, transforming how consumers save and how retailers connect with their customers. Our app is used by millions of members, helping them reduce everyday costs while delivering measurable value back to our retail partners.




We work with a wide range of well-known brands, using our proprietary technology, real-time transaction data and advanced analytics to power smarter, more effective performance marketing. By combining data, insight and creativity, we enable retailers to better understand and engage their customers, driving incremental revenue and long-term loyalty.




Our platform delivers a seamless, best-in-class experience for both members and partners, and we continue to invest in new features and capabilities to support our growth and ambition.




At Airtime, we are driven by innovation and this is what keeps us ahead. We are a team with a shared ambition to succeed, holding ourselves to high standards while working collaboratively to achieve our goals




The Opportunity




Airtime is looking for a Data Analyst with 3+ years of experience in a data analytics role. This is an opportunity for someone who can move beyond basic reporting and play a meaningful role in driving partner performance and commercial decisions across the business.




You’ll work across multiple areas including Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance, giving you broad exposure to how the business operates. Alongside supporting ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, you’ll focus on shaping problems, uncovering insights, and delivering clear, actionable recommendations that support strategy and performance.




Reporting to the Analytics Manager, you will:




Analyse performance data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across multiple areas of the business
Partner with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance to shape problems and deliver actionable insights that drive decision-making
Translate business questions into structured analysis and clear, actionable recommendations
Communicate complex data through clear, compelling narratives tailored to both technical and non-technical audiences
Develop a strong understanding of Airtime’s product, platform, and commercial model to provide context-rich analysis
Proactively identify opportunities to optimise performance, improve processes, and support business growth
Support ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, ensuring insights are consistent and relevant
Contribute to improving data quality, definitions, and consistency across the business




Requirements:

3+ years of experience in a data analytics role
Experience working cross-functionally and building relationships with stakeholders across different business areas
Strong commercial awareness and understanding of how data impacts business performance
Experience translating business requests or problems into structured analysis and actionable recommendations
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience working with large and complex datasets
Proficiency in SQL (BigQuery a plus), Excel, and data visualisation tools
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with experience presenting insights and influencing decision-making
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Curious, proactive, and motivated to use data to drive improvements
Able to commute to the Manchester office twice per week




What You Will Get

We believe great work happens when people feel supported, trusted, and genuinely valued. So we’ve built a benefits package that’s designed to give you flexibility, support your wellbeing, and help you grow, both inside and outside of work.




Reward & Security

Share options, be part of our success
Competitive salary
Life assurance at 5x your salary




Time Off That Matters

23 days holiday, plus more with every year you’re here (up to 28)
Your birthday off, have a ‘you day’
Buy extra holiday when you need it (up to 5 extra days)
A dedicated charity day to give back




Flexibility & Balance

Flexible start times (anytime between 6:30–10:30am)
Hybrid working to balance home and office life
A modern, central office space when you want to collaborate in person




Health & Wellbeing

Private medical insurance
Health cash plan for everyday healthcare
Virtual GP access for you and your family
24/7 support for mental health, counselling, and wellbeing




Growth & Development

Learning & development budget and dedicated time to use it
Professional accreditation funding
Real opportunities to learn from experienced colleagues and grow your career




Support Through Life

Enhanced maternity, paternity & adoption leave
A culture that understands life happens outside of work




Community & Culture

Company charity contributions
Regular team moments that actually feel meaningful (not forced fun)
A team that values collaboration, curiosity, and getting things done




The Bottom Line

We’re building a place where you can do your best work without burning out, standing still, or feeling like just another cog in the machine.




Because when you’re supported, everything else follows.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Aircraft Health / Predictive Maintenance)




Location: Filton (60% onsite)

Contract: Until 31/12/2026 (possible extension)

Hours: 35 hours per week (4.5 days, flexible between 7am–7pm)

Rate: £50.00 per hour Umbrella / £37.38 per hour PAYE

Security Clearance: BPSS+ (to be completed prior to start)

IR35: Inside IR35




About the Role

Our client is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support advanced aircraft fleet analytics, focusing on predictive maintenance and diagnostic modelling. This is a fantastic opportunity to work within a highly technical environment, contributing to improvements in fleet performance and operational efficiency.




Key Responsibilities of the Data Analyst

Develop and enhance fleet diagnostic and predictive maintenance models
Analyse aircraft in-service data to support technical investigations and design improvements
Work closely with stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver data-driven studies and insights [
Support wider data management and analytics initiatives across the business
Improve understanding of in-service operations and system performance through data analysis




Essential Skills & Experience

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

Location: Bristol (Hybrid - 2–3 days per week in office)

Industry: SaaS

Salary: £50,000 – £60,000 DOE




The Opportunity

I’m currently partnering with a fast-growing SaaS business based in Bristol that is looking to hire a Data Analyst to join their expanding data function.




This role offers the chance to work at the heart of a product-driven organisation, where data is central to shaping customer experience, product development and commercial strategy. You’ll collaborate with teams across product, marketing, customer success and engineering to deliver insights that directly influence business growth and user engagement.




The company has built a strong data culture and is continuing to invest heavily in its analytics capabilities, making this an ideal opportunity for someone who wants to have real impact while developing their technical skillset.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse product, customer and commercial data to uncover trends, behaviours and opportunities for growth.
Build and maintain dashboards and visualisations to track KPIs across product usage, customer engagement and revenue performance.
Work closely with stakeholders to understand business challenges and translate them into data-driven solutions.
Support the development and optimisation of data models and pipelines to ensure reliable and scalable reporting.
Deliver insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Contribute to improving data quality, governance, and analytics best practices across the organisation.




Essential Skills & Experience

Proven experience working as a Data Analyst, ideally within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Strong SQL skills with experience querying large, complex datasets.
Hands-on experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
Experience using Python for data analysis and manipulation.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.




Desirable Skills

Experience working with product analytics, customer journey data or subscription-based metrics (e.g. churn, LTV, CAC).

Experience collaborating with product or engineering teams in an agile environment.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate data into clear business insights.




Package & Benefits

£50,000 - £60,000 salary (depending on experience)
Hybrid working – 2–3 days per week in the Bristol office
Annual bonus scheme
Private healthcare
Pension contribution
25 days annual leave plus bank holidays
Learning & development budget and career progression opportunities

Title: Data Analyst - TikTok LIVE
Description:
Responsibilities
The Regional LIVE Operation Strategy Team leads various cross-functional projects. Our key initiatives include developing strategic plans to drive widespread adoption of LIVE at scale among creators, localizing new product features, creating user engagement strategies, ensuring a positive creator experience through education on the Community Guidelines and best practices, and providing ad hoc analysis and support to cross-functional partners.

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our dynamic LIVE team. In this role, you’ll uncover insights and deliver data-driven recommendations that directly impact how we grow and support our creator ecosystem. You'll work cross-functionally with product, strategy, and operations teams to shape our growth priorities using data and analytical thinking.

- Analyze local and global content and user trends to identify growth opportunities within the EU LIVE ecosystem.
- Create, automate, and maintain reports and dashboards to track performance, OKRs, and key strategic initiatives.
- Develop data models and perform in-depth analyses to support content strategy, creator segmentation, and user engagement.
- Translate complex data into actionable insights for regional teams and leadership.
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (e.g., Creator Management, Product, Strategy) to inform decision-making.
- Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders to influence business strategy.

Examples of Problems You’ll Be Solving
- What are the best content verticals for us to focus our creator recruitment on?
- How can we build a predictive model to identify high-potential creators early?
- What are the right leading and lagging indicators to measure success against our key OKRs?
- How can we segment creators and users to better personalize support and growth strategies?
- When and how should we educate creators to maximize their long-term retention and performance?
- How can we simplify complex data sets using statistical tools to better diagnose issues and recommend scalable solutions?

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications
- 5+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in a high-growth or tech environment.
- Strong mathematical and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret large datasets and generate actionable insights.
- Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python preferred).
- Solid understanding of statistics and data modeling techniques.
- Experience using data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.
- High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience working in a consumer technology, content, or creator economy business.
- Background in building predictive models or recommendation systems.
- Familiarity with A/B testing and experimental design.
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics for non-technical audiences.
- Knowledge of the live streaming industry or digital content ecosystems is a plus.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
JOB TITLE: Junior Analyst

REPORTS TO: Senior Business Analyst

DEPARTMENT: Support

LOCATION: Penta Consulting Ltd, Oaks House, 16-22 West Street, Epsom, Surrey, KT18 7RG




ABOUT PENTA CONSULTING LTD




At Penta Consulting, we believe technology reaches its full potential when powered by the right people. As a leading provider of Technology Resource Solutions, we connect businesses with top-tier talent to drive digital transformation and innovation.




With over 25 years of experience, we’ve built a global reputation for delivering high-quality expertise across key areas such as software development, artificial intelligence, project management, cloud computing, cybersecurity, enterprise architecture, and data analytics.




Our clients span the globe, relying on our team of specialists to deliver tailored, impactful solutions. We’re proud to be a trusted partner in helping organisations stay ahead in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.




If you're looking to join an award-winning, forward-thinking, and solution-focused team, Penta Consulting is the place to grow your career.




KEY STAKEHOLDERS




Executive Management, Senior Leadership, Professional Services Department, Resource Operations Department, Company Operations, International Team




JOB PURPOSE




This person will play an active role in maintaining the best practices and adherence to our internal playbook.




DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES




This person will be responsible for:




Data Integrity & Governance

Conduct scheduled data quality checks within Mercury CRM, including:
Mandatory field validation
Vacancies Open for more than 6 months
Reason code completeness
Duplicate and inconsistent record checks
Detection of backdated changes
Support enforcement of standardised definitions, dropdowns, and workflows across teams.
Log and escalate data integrity risks to the Senior Business Analyst.




Mercury CRM Workflow Compliance

Monitor user adherence to workflows across Sales, Care, Operations, and Compliance in line with Mercury playbook
Surface issues such as missing paperwork, incorrect placement statuses, and overdue approvals.
Maintain compliance dashboards and provide weekly summaries of key findings.




Departmental Reporting Support

Own the operational data inputs for departmental dashboards, including:
Business performance (Frequency)
Trend analysis
Compliance tasks
Report on company performance
Validate accuracy of departmental submissions and ensure alignment with reporting standards.
Identifying deficiencies withing the database and the CRM systems




Architectural Alignment

Coordinate between frontline systems (Mercury CRM, Business Central) and downstream models (Power BI).
Track, document, and communicate system issues or enhancements for BI and Mercury development teams.
Support change management as reporting logic, rules, or calculations evolve.




Documentation & Change Control

Maintain all process documentation related to reporting, data controls, and audit routines.
Manage version control for reporting logic, definitions, and workflows.
Create clear summaries of issues, resolutions, and schema or field changes.




EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS




Strong background in operational reporting, data quality, or business operations.
High attention to detail and accuracy; confident validating complex datasets.
Experience working with CRM or ERP systems (Mercury CRM or Business Central advantageous).
Competent in Excel; familiarity with Power BI preferred.
Ability to manage recurring reporting cycles with precision and consistency.
Strong communication skills for cross functional collaboration.




KEY COMPETENCIES




Analytical thinking - ability to identify inconsistencies, anomalies, and patterns in data.
Process ownership -takes responsibility for operational routines and service-level commitments.
Cross-functional coordination - collaborates effectively across Sales, Delivery, Finance, and Compliance.
Proactive problem solving -identifies issues early and escalates with solutions.




THE IDEAL CANDIDATE




High data quality in Mercury CRM and departmental submissions.
Well maintained templates and reporting processes.
Clear documentation and traceability of all reporting changes.

The Data Analyst role will support the TfL Mechanical & Electrical contract to support contractual performance and data consistency. Key Responsibilities


Data entry
Data processing
Data analysis and reporting.
Consolidating data.
Understand the current systems and work towards improving the data relationships between different systems.
Reviewing data collection processes and assessing the validity of the information stored

Working closely with the relevant teams to ensure that the data collection and storage systems are secure and robust.
Create interactive reports & dashboards with Power BI

Required Skills And Experience


Microsoft Excel High level of skill and experience
Collect, review, analyse and maintain contract performance data and use it to support contract delivery.

Identify and implement quality and efficiency improvement initiatives. Identifying inefficiencies across the contract.
Assist in the development of the innovation plan for service delivery improvements.
Engage business leaders and senior operational managers across the business to ensure best practice and operational efficiencies are implemented.
Understand, anticipate and deliver customer (internal and external) needs while building effective relationships.
Efficiently respond to both internal and external customers through effective communication and personal accessibility.
Ensure customer satisfaction is high.
Carry out other admin tasks as directed.

Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate Data Analyst Opportunity – Launch Your Career in Data, Business Insight, and Digital Transformation




24-Month Fixed Term Contract | Bristol | £26,000 (Year 1)




Are you a technically-minded graduate eager to launch your career in the dynamic world of data, business improvement, and IT transformation? Here’s your chance to join ea Change, a leading firm dedicated to developing the next generation of business and IT leaders.




About Us

ea Change has over two decades of expertise driving Business and IT transformation. We help organisations achieve meaningful, lasting change while nurturing the leaders of tomorrow through our Futures Programme. Our culture prioritises diversity, inclusion, integrity, and people-first values, ensuring every team member thrives.




The Role

As a Graduate Data Analyst, you will be a key contributor on client projects, collaborating with senior staff, supporting decision-making, and helping drive business improvement. No two days are the same – your work will have real impact, turning data into actionable insight across a range of business areas.




Responsibilities include:

Analyse and interpret data to provide actionable business insights.
Create compelling visualisations using tools such as Power BI, PowerApps, and other BI or visualization platforms.
Support business improvement initiatives with data-driven recommendations.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand and define business requirements.
Assist in maintaining and optimising databases using SQL or other data management tools.
Contribute to project documentation, reporting, and stakeholder communications.




We welcome graduates with technical backgrounds who may already have some of these skills, or who are proficient in other analytical, coding, or visualization tools and are eager to learn and grow.




Requirements

A 2:1 in a technical undergraduate degree.
Right to work in the United Kingdom.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and numerical skills.
Excellent communication and collaboration abilities.
Strong attention to detail and a proactive mindset.
Full driving licence and ability to travel to the office as needed (hybrid working).
Experience or familiarity with SQL, Power BI, PowerApps, Python, R, Excel, Tableau, or similar tools is a plus but not essential.




Benefits

Fully funded accredited qualifications
Bespoke flexible training
A health cash plan
26 days annual leave
Regular mentoring and personal development
Access to a comprehensive learning portal
Opportunities for progression and a robust fast-track into Business Change
Hybrid working and commercial exposure




At ea Change, you will find a vibrant community that wholeheartedly believes in pushing boundaries, embracing innovation, and celebrating diversity. We assure an ambience where everyone is encouraged to express themselves, contributing to our shared goal of shaping the future of business success.




How to Apply

If you're interested in this opportunity and fit the bill, we'd be thrilled to hear from you so kindly submit your CV. Together, let's shape the future!




ea Change are unable to sponsor work visas for international applicants as we are not on the UK Border Agency's Sponsor Register.




If we are fortunate in receiving a high volume of quality applications we may not be able to get back to everyone individually. Please assume if you have not heard back from the team within 14 days of application, your application has been unsuccessful.
