Title: Executive Assistant to Chief People Officer
Description:
Executive Assistant to CPO




Contract: Permanent

Location: Nottingham

Recruitment Partner: Anastasia Walker







We are looking for an experienced, commercially minded EA to support our Chief People Officer — someone who can go beyond coordination and actively drive outcomes. This role sits at the centre of our People function and plays a critical part in enabling strategic delivery across the business.




You will operate as a trusted partner to the CPO, bringing structure, pace and clarity to priorities. Acting as an extension of the CPO, you will help shape agendas, drive actions, and ensure the People Leadership Team (PLT) are aligned.




What you’ll be doing

Delivering best-in-class executive support — anticipating needs, solving problems, and driving execution. In addition you will;

Act as a true right hand to the CPO, ensuring focus on the highest-value priorities
Drive follow-through on PLT actions, maintaining momentum and holding stakeholders to account
Create high-quality presentations and materials for Board and executive-level discussions
Translate ideas into structured outputs — turning strategy into clear, actionable plans
Identify issues early and proactively drive solutions, not just escalate problems
Bring discipline to time, priorities and decision-making across the CPO’s agenda
Act as a connector across the organisation, ensuring alignment and flow of information




Key responsibilities

Serve as the primary interface for the CPO, engaging senior stakeholders across the organisation
Proactively manage and optimise the CPO’s time, ensuring alignment to strategic priorities
Prepare high-impact presentations, briefings and executive materials to a consistently high standard
Drive governance and rhythm for the People Leadership Team, including tracking actions and outcomes
Manage complex scheduling, travel and logistics
Build strong relationships across EA/ PA, and leadership communities to enable delivery at pace
Respond to business-critical queries with sound judgement and commercial awareness
Support delivery of key People initiatives by coordinating activity and ensuring execution




What you’ll need to have (our must-haves)

Proven experience supporting a CPO, HR Director or senior executive in a large, matrixed organisation
Advanced Microsoft 365 capability — able to build clear, compelling, executive-level narratives
Strong commercial judgement with the ability to prioritise based on business impact
A highly proactive approach — you anticipate, challenge and drive outcomes
Experience operating in a fast-paced, high-expectation environment with shifting priorities
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with confidence engaging at senior levels
Strong written and verbal communication, with attention to detail and clarity
High levels of discretion, professionalism and trust







It would be great if you also have

Experience operating in a strategic support role
Track record of contributing to projects or transformation initiatives

Background supporting corporate functions within a retail or similarly complex organisation







Where your brilliance can take you

At Boots, this role offers real exposure and influence. You will be part of shaping how the People function delivers against business priorities, with opportunities to grow your scope and impact over time.




Rewards designed for you

Boots Retirement Savings Plan (up to 12% company contribution)
Generous employee discounts for you and a family member
Enhanced maternity/paternity/adoption leave pay
Access to free, 24/7 counselling and support through TELUS Health, our Employee Assistance Programme.

There’s lots more in our benefits and discounts, MyBoosts – there to give you that little lift in your everyday. Find out more at http://boots.jobs/rewards. Exclusions may apply; eligible roles only.




A bit about us

At Boots, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer, creating a place where everyone feels welcome, supported, and free to be themselves. We believe that when our people feel valued and included, they thrive, so we're committed to creating a brilliant Boots for our people so they can make a difference for our customers and colleagues every time.




What’s next?

If you apply, our team will be in touch to let you know the outcome of your application or to arrange next steps. Where a role is advertised as full-time, we are open to discussing part-time and job share options. If you require additional support as part of the application and interview process, we are happy to provide reasonable adjustments to help you be at your best.




We hope to hear from you soon.

Be brilliant with Boots.

Title: Data Coordinator
Description:
Job Description

We're looking for a Data Coordinator to work closely with team members to identify data needs and requirements, develop and implement processes to improve data quality and efficiency, and provide organisational and administrative support to the team.

The role

As a Data Coordinator, you’ll play an important role within our IT – Data and Architecture team, collaborating with colleagues to drive improvements in data quality and operational efficiency. You'll assist the Data Leads in coordinating the Data team's workload and provide support to legal team members as needed. You'll also stay up to date with industry trends and best practices related to data management, helping to keep the team at the forefront of the field.

What You'll Be Doing



Ensuring all data is accurate and up to date and developing and implementing processes to improve data quality and efficiency.
Collaborating with various stakeholders to identify data needs and requirements, conducting data analysis, and generating reports as required.
Coordinating meetings and supporting documentation and assisting the Data Leads in the coordination of the Data team's workload.

Title: Menopause Specialist
Description:
Established in 2020, we are a warm, patient-focused boutique menopause clinic in Nottingham

looking for an experienced and compassionate Menopause Specialist to join our growing team. CQC

rated Good.




This is an excellent opportunity for a GP with a special interest in menopause care, or a clinician with

advanced experience in women’s health, to work in a supportive and flexible private practice environment where patient care comes first.




About the Role

You will provide personalised menopause and perimenopause consultations, helping patients

manage symptoms and improve their quality of life through evidence-based, holistic care.




The role offers:

Flexible clinic sessions (in person and video)
Private patient appointments with adequate consultation times
Administrative support
A collaborative and friendly clinical environment
Opportunity to help shape and grow the menopause service




Responsibilities

Conduct comprehensive menopause and women’s health assessments
Develop individualised treatment plans, including HRT where appropriate
Provide ongoing follow-up and lifestyle guidance
Maintain accurate clinical records and high standards of patient care
Work collaboratively with the wider clinic team




The Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who is:

GMC registered and licensed to practice
Experienced in menopause management and women’s health
Ideally holding recognised menopause training or accreditation
Empathetic, approachable and patient-centred
Comfortable working independently in a private clinic setting
Fits well within a small, inclusive and respectful team culture
Available to start as soon as possible




What we offer

Competitive sessional rates
Flexible working arrangements
Supportive and professional team culture
Modern clinic facilities in Nottingham (postcode location)
Opportunity for long-term growth within the practice




If you are passionate about improving women’s health and want to be part of a clinic that values

compassionate, high-quality care, we would love to hear from you.




To apply or arrange an informal conversation, please contact us with your CV and availability by

emailing us at info@healthinmenopause.co.uk or leave a message for Dr Alice Duffy on 0115

6463390.

manage symptoms and improve their quality of life through evidence-based, holistic care.


The role offers:

  • Flexible clinic sessions (in person and video)
  • Private patient appointments with adequate consultation times
  • Administrative support
  • A collaborative and friendly clinical environment
  • Opportunity to help shape and grow the menopause service


Responsibilities

  • Conduct comprehensive menopause and women’s health assessments
  • Develop individualised treatment plans, including HRT where appropriate
  • Provide ongoing follow-up and lifestyle guidance
  • Maintain accurate clinical records and high standards of patient care
  • Work collaboratively with the wider clinic team


The Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who is:

  • GMC registered and licensed to practice
  • Experienced in menopause management and women’s health
  • Ideally holding recognised menopause training or accreditation
  • Empathetic, approachable and patient-centred
  • Comfortable working independently in a private clinic setting
  • Fits well within a small, inclusive and respectful team culture
  • Available to start as soon as possible


What we offer

  • Competitive sessional rates
  • Flexible working arrangements
  • Supportive and professional team culture
  • Modern clinic facilities in Nottingham (postcode location)
  • Opportunity for long-term growth within the practice


If you are passionate about improving women’s health and want to be part of a clinic that values

compassionate, high-quality care, we would love to hear from you.


To apply or arrange an informal conversation, please contact us with your CV and availability by

emailing us at info@healthinmenopause.co.uk or leave a message for Dr Alice Duffy on 0115

6463390.

Title: Data Business Partner
Description:
Data Business Partner – Central Nottingham / Hybrid

£50,000 - £56,000 + bonus, 35-hour work week and great benefits

This profitable and growing business are looking to add an experienced Data Business Partner to their collaborative team. The ideal candidate will have good knowledge of the principles and best practice in MI development. This role leans itself to be more of a ‘Business Data Analyst role’, where you will act as the bridge between the data team and wider business stakeholders.

The Data Business Partner will still undertake hands on data analysis, but you will work closely with clients and central marketing teams to provide insight and guidance on how data and MI can add value. A technical understanding is expected, but the key to this role is having the personality to develop strong links with leadership teams and become their trusted advisor within the data team.

You will develop the data strategy for clients to align with business priorities and their needs whilst providing value through data and MI.

The role will require 1-2 days per week in the office, with the ideal location being their head office in Nottingham. Birmingham or London could also be considered.

Package


Bonus opportunities
35-hour work week with flexible working
25 days holiday + 5 days buy/sell + bank holidays.
Professional development opportunities
5% employer pension, rising with service + many more.


Key Requirements


Excellent communication skills and the ability to communicate complex data to non-technical audiences.
Experience working with internal stakeholders / clients to understand their data journey and how value can be provided through data and MI
Experience designing, developing and maintaining MI Solutions e.g. reporting, dashboards and analytics tools
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience of working in an analytical and data intensive environment
Tech Stack – SSRS, SSMS, PowerBI, T-SQL, SQL Server, Visual Studio


The company have an excellent reputation within their sector, and have experienced 14 consecutive years of growth, posting record revenues for the last financial year. They promote a healthy work-life balance and will give you the opportunity to develop your technical knowledge.

Click APPLY to be considered for the role as my client is aiming to interview as soon as possible. All interviews are to be conducted virtually, with the process requiring a maximum of two stages.

Title: Board Trustee
Description:
Company Description

Headway Nottingham is a specialist brain injury organisation serving individuals across Nottingham and Nottinghamshire who have been affected by brain injury. Our core service is a social rehabilitation day centre offering workshops that address complex physical, cognitive, and behavioural challenges, alongside practical support with welfare, housing, and advocacy through our Casework service. Our By the Bedside project collaborates with the Neuro Trauma Service at Queen's Medical Centre to assist patients and their families in hospital during the initial stages of their brain injury journey. As an independent local charity affiliated with Headway UK, we have been an "Approved Provider of Day Services" for Nottingham City and Nottinghamshire County Councils since 2011. We are committed to supporting individuals with acquired brain injuries in achieving fulfilling lives beyond their disabilities.




Role Description

This is a part-time, volunteer role based in Nottinghamshire. As a Board Trustee, you will provide strategic guidance, governance, and oversight to ensure the charity fulfils its mission and complies with its legal duties. You will attend 6 board meetings each year, with occasional extra meeting if you join a sub-committee. Trustees act as ambassadors for Headway Nottingham, fostering meaningful relationships with stakeholders, assisting in fundraising efforts, and supporting the leadership team in delivering impactful services to the community.




Specifics

We are particularly looking for those with a skillset which we feel will add to, and compliment our current Board. With this in mind, we are currently looking for those with with experience in marketing/PR, IT, fundraising and impact measurement/evaluation. Of course, if you feel you have a skillset not mentioned here that would be useful to us please get in touch!




Qualifications/Experience

Experience in governance, strategic planning, or non-profit management is desirable.
Strong decision-making, critical thinking, and leadership skills.
Proficiency in effective communication, advocacy, and stakeholder engagement.
Passion for supporting individuals with disabilities and promoting equity and inclusion.
A commitment to the vision and values of Headway Nottingham, including adherence to its code of conduct and confidentiality policies.




Application

We value receiving a CV and covering letter detailing how your skillset will positively impact out Board and why you are interested in joining us.

Title: AI Training Experts (Freelance - Nottingham)
Description:
AI Training Experts

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We're looking for AI Training Experts to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models. If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models.

Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay up to of $25/hr per AI task completed. You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring


AI Training task skills and verifiable professional experience as an AI Trainer
A willingness to take our skills verification test to assess your suitability for our participant pool
Strong attention to detail and the ability to concentrate on complex tasks for up to one hour at a time
A reliable and fast internet connection and access to a computer
A willingness to self-declare your earnings, as our participants are self-employed
A Paypal account to receive payment from our clients


What you'll be doing in the role


Completing AI training tasks such as analyzing, editing, and writing annotations
Judging the performance of AI in performing
Improving cutting-edge AI models


Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links to more information on Prolific

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
At MHR, our employees are central to our success and play a key role in helping customers achieve sustainable high performance. With a team of over 900 professionals, we work to make things flow smoothly, whether it's for large organisations or individual employees. As businesses face rapid changes in the world of work, our team is here to help them adapt and thrive.




By focusing on the core needs of efficiency, productivity, growth, and impact, our employees use their expertise to deliver real solutions through our People and Finance platform. This system, which covers finance, HR, payroll, and learning, helps businesses run more smoothly and make better decisions in real time.




With over 40 years of experience behind us, MHR’s track record as a high-performance organisation is built on clear goals, a shared vision, and strong communication - all of which we pass on to our customers.




MHR is more than just a place to work; it’s a platform for empowerment. Joining us means bringing innovation, technology, and teamwork seamlessly removes obstacles, enhances your skills, and allow you to focus on what’s most important to you- work that matters.




With us, you’ll grow, find your flow, and make a lasting difference in your career, your team, and your impact.




Your Career

At MHR, your career is shaped by curiosity, innovation, and the drive to build solutions that make a real difference. As a Data Engineer, you’ll join a team where your technical skills will grow rapidly through hands‑on delivery, exposure to modern data engineering practices, and collaboration with experts across cloud, software engineering, and data architecture.




You’ll work with advanced SQL, Python, Azure PaaS, and modern data frameworks, while developing your understanding of analytics architecture, machine learning engineering, and large‑scale data platform design. This role offers a clear pathway to senior engineering opportunities as you deepen your expertise and take ownership of increasingly complex solutions.




Your Team

You’ll be part of a collaborative, forward‑thinking data engineering function responsible for powering PeopleFirst, our flagship HR and Payroll platform. Working closely with Cloud Platform, Data Architects, Security, Software Engineering, and Product teams, you’ll help shape the data foundations that underpin our products and customer experience.




Your team champions modern data practices, continuous learning, and a culture of problem‑solving. You’ll contribute to a supportive environment where ideas are shared openly, innovation is encouraged, and everyone plays a role in driving our technology forward.




Your Impact

In this role, you’ll play a key part in designing, building, and optimising the data platforms, pipelines, marts, and warehouses that enable PeopleFirst to deliver secure, scalable, high‑quality data experiences.




Your work will include:

Developing robust data solutions using SQL, Python, and Azure technologies such as Data Factory, Databricks, Azure Functions, Synapse, and more
Designing and optimising data structures, storage, and query performance to support analytics, reporting, and product features
Contributing to the delivery of new data platforms and features for both core (iTrent/PeopleFirst) and non‑core product areas.
Collaborating with cross‑functional teams to ensure data is secure, compliant, and aligned with GDPR requirements.
Supporting the development of BI and ML engineering practices across the business.
Delivering proof‑of‑concepts that push our technology stack and ways of working forward.
Helping internal teams with data modelling, query design, and best‑practice engineering approaches.




Benefits to support you personally and professionally




Alongside the opportunity to work with an incredible team and express your individuality, we offer a range of personalised benefits including:

Access to over 60 internal training courses, professional qualifications, and cross-departmental mentoring and coaching programs
24/7 access to a GP through Help at Hand for you and your family, confidential mental health support, and paid sick leave
Ability to purchase or sell additional holiday days beyond 25 days and bank holidays
Enhanced Family leave
My MHRewards offering discounts at over 900 retailers
Monthly employee recognition program and departmental awards
Referral bonus scheme of up to £2,000
Employee-led social events such as running clubs, football teams, book clubs, and bake-offs
On-site subsidised restaurants offering a variety of fresh meals daily
Opt-in benefits schemes such as private medical insurance and dental coverage




We value the well-being, financial stability, and inclusivity of our employees, and strive to provide a supportive working environment.




At MHR, we’re completely committed to creating a supportive and inclusive workplace where all voices are heard, and everyone feels valued and supported.




Whatever your background, abilities and experiences, if you’re excited by this role we want to hear from you. If you require assistance for an interview, please let us know and we’ll make sure your needs are met when you arrive.




If we receive an exceptionally high volume of applications, we reserve the right to close this vacancy earlier than originally advertised.

Title: Trainee Wealth Planner
Description:
Position: Trainee Wealth Planner - Entry Level

Company Overview

Hoxton Wealth is a fast-growing, global financial services firm operating across Cyprus, the US, UK, Dubai, South Africa, Australia and Asia. Managing over £4 billion in assets, we combine cutting-edge technology with tax-led financial planning to deliver smarter, more efficient wealth solutions for our clients.

Our approach is simple: modern tools, expert advice and a global mindset. We help clients navigate complex cross-border financial needs, build long-term wealth and protect what matters most.

About The Opportunity

Location: Dubai (relocation required) work at the centre of a global business in one of the world's most dynamic financial hubs.

This is not your typical graduate role, it is a high-performance career launchpad into international wealth management and it is not for everyone.

As a Trainee Wealth Manager, you will start by mastering one of the most valuable skills in finance: generating opportunity. You will learn how to build relationships, create deal flow and develop a pipeline of high-value clients across Hoxton's specialist areas.

From day one, you will be immersed in the industry, gaining exposure to investment strategy, financial planning, client advisory and technical structuring. This is a hands-on role where performance drives progression.

You will begin your journey in Dubai, working alongside experienced professionals in a fast-paced, ambitious environment, while completing a structured 24–36 month training programme. This includes earning UK-recognised financial qualifications and preparing to step into a fully-fledged advisory role within one of our global offices.

For the right person, this role offers rapid career acceleration, international mobility and uncapped earning potential.

What You Will Be Doing


Engaging with prospective clients to understand their current financial situation, goals, and challenges
Having meaningful conversations to identify whether Hoxton's services can add value
Booking qualified meetings for senior advisors based on genuine client needs and suitability
Building trust and long-term relationships through professional, insightful communication
Developing your knowledge of financial planning, investment solutions, and cross-border structuring
Supporting advisors in delivering tailored strategies aligned to each client's objectives
Working towards becoming a fully qualified Financial Planner



This is a target-driven role where consistency is key, you will be expected to secure at least one qualified meeting per day as a minimum standard.

What We Are Looking For

We're not focused on hiring experience, we are hiring mindset.


Resilience: You stay consistent, professional and motivated while building relationships and developing opportunities over time
Drive: You're competitive and motivated to outperform
Work ethic: You are prepared to put in the hours to get results
Communication: Confident, clear and professional on the phone
Curiosity: You are eager to learn and develop new skills quickly
Discipline: You can stick to structure, targets and routines
Initiative: You don't wait to be told - you take action
Professionalism: You present yourself well and communicate with credibility



Compensation & Progression

Year 1


Salary: 9,000 AED/month
Commission: 5% up to £100k, 10% above (rolling 12 months)
Benefits: Visa + medical insurance



Year 2


Salary: 10,000 AED/month
Commission: 10% above £100k
Benefits: Visa + medical insurance



Year 3


Salary: 11,000 AED/month
Commission: 10% above £100k
Benefits: Visa + medical insurance



Performance Benchmarks


5+ hours talk time per week
5+ booked meetings
3+ completed meetings

Position: Trainee Wealth Planner - Entry Level

Company Overview

Hoxton Wealth is a fast-growing, global financial services firm operating across Cyprus, the US, UK, Dubai, South Africa, Australia and Asia. Managing over £4 billion in assets, we combine cutting-edge technology with tax-led financial planning to deliver smarter, more efficient wealth solutions for our clients.

Our approach is simple: modern tools, expert advice and a global mindset. We help clients navigate complex cross-border financial needs, build long-term wealth and protect what matters most.

About The Opportunity

Location: Dubai (relocation required) work at the centre of a global business in one of the world's most dynamic financial hubs.

This is not your typical graduate role, it is a high-performance career launchpad into international wealth management and it is not for everyone.

As a Trainee Wealth Manager, you will start by mastering one of the most valuable skills in finance: generating opportunity. You will learn how to build relationships, create deal flow and develop a pipeline of high-value clients across Hoxton's specialist areas.

From day one, you will be immersed in the industry, gaining exposure to investment strategy, financial planning, client advisory and technical structuring. This is a hands-on role where performance drives progression.

You will begin your journey in Dubai, working alongside experienced professionals in a fast-paced, ambitious environment, while completing a structured 24–36 month training programme. This includes earning UK-recognised financial qualifications and preparing to step into a fully-fledged advisory role within one of our global offices.

For the right person, this role offers rapid career acceleration, international mobility and uncapped earning potential.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Engaging with prospective clients to understand their current financial situation, goals, and challenges
  • Having meaningful conversations to identify whether Hoxton's services can add value
  • Booking qualified meetings for senior advisors based on genuine client needs and suitability
  • Building trust and long-term relationships through professional, insightful communication
  • Developing your knowledge of financial planning, investment solutions, and cross-border structuring
  • Supporting advisors in delivering tailored strategies aligned to each client's objectives
  • Working towards becoming a fully qualified Financial Planner


This is a target-driven role where consistency is key, you will be expected to secure at least one qualified meeting per day as a minimum standard.

What We Are Looking For

We're not focused on hiring experience, we are hiring mindset.

  • Resilience: You stay consistent, professional and motivated while building relationships and developing opportunities over time
  • Drive: You're competitive and motivated to outperform
  • Work ethic: You are prepared to put in the hours to get results
  • Communication: Confident, clear and professional on the phone
  • Curiosity: You are eager to learn and develop new skills quickly
  • Discipline: You can stick to structure, targets and routines
  • Initiative: You don't wait to be told - you take action
  • Professionalism: You present yourself well and communicate with credibility


Compensation & Progression

Year 1

  • Salary: 9,000 AED/month
  • Commission: 5% up to £100k, 10% above (rolling 12 months)
  • Benefits: Visa + medical insurance


Year 2

  • Salary: 10,000 AED/month
  • Commission: 10% above £100k
  • Benefits: Visa + medical insurance


Year 3

  • Salary: 11,000 AED/month
  • Commission: 10% above £100k
  • Benefits: Visa + medical insurance


Performance Benchmarks

  • 5+ hours talk time per week
  • 5+ booked meetings
  • 3+ completed meetings


Progression Targets

  • 3 months: £1M AUM / 4 clients
  • 6 months: £3M AUM / 6 clients / £40k revenue
  • Year 1: £6M AUM / 16 clients / £100k revenue
  • Year 2: £6M AUM / 25 clients / £200k revenue
  • Year 3: £7.5M AUM / 30 clients / £250k revenue


Career Path

Successful completion of the programme leads to a Pathway Financial Planner role, a fully salaried advisory position with continued support as you transition into managing your own clients.
